AWEIL Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

Selection for all posts will be based on shortlisting from applications received, followed by a Personal Interview conducted by a duly constituted selection committee. There is no written examination. Shortlisting criteria may be enhanced by the Competent Authority if a large number of candidates fulfil the eligibility criteria. The decision of AWEIL on all matters related to eligibility, shortlisting and selection shall be final and binding.

Stage Process Applicable Posts
Stage 1 Shortlisting based on Educational Qualification and Experience as per notification criteria All Posts
Stage 2 Personal Interview by a duly constituted selection committee All Posts

AWEIL Recruitment 2026 - Post-wise Scope of Work

Each post carries a defined scope of responsibility at AWEIL Corporate Office, Kanpur. The selected candidate may also be deputed to any AWEIL unit or Government office as per requirement.

Post Key Responsibilities
Sr. Manager (IT) Overseeing IT infrastructure at AWEIL HQ; leading IT staff; MIS development; server maintenance; LAN and communication monitoring; IT asset management; ERP technical support; website monitoring
Executive (Corporate Communication) Social media management; digital content & poster creation; event management; stakeholder communication; brand building; advertisement; media coverage monitoring; crisis communication; internal communications; protocol and coordination
Legal Expert Drafting and vetting legal documents, agreements, MOUs, JV Agreements, EOI & RFP conditions, lease agreements, bye-laws; rendering legal opinions on corporate, contractual, service, disciplinary, taxation and arbitration matters; handling legal notices; appearing and defending AWEIL before Courts/Tribunals/Forums/Statutory Bodies
Executive (Official Language) Implementation of Official Language Policy of the Government of India; translation of official correspondence, scientific literature and documents from English to Hindi and vice versa; preparation of Official Language reports and correspondence
Jr. Executive (HR) HR strategy implementation; recruitment, selection, promotion and performance appraisal; compliance with Labour Laws; RTI; liaison with enforcement agencies; welfare services for employees/ex-employees; CSR initiatives; grievance redressal; implementation of Government policies; handling court cases

Who should NOT apply for AWEIL Recruitment 2026?

  • Candidates whose age exceeds the upper limit for their respective post as on 15-06-2026, even by one day.
  • Legal Expert applicants who do not hold LLB with minimum 60% marks or who are not registered in Bar Council anywhere in India.
  • Executive (Official Language) applicants who do not have a Master's degree meeting the Hindi-English language medium combinations specified and who do not have 03 years' translation/terminological work experience.
  • Executive (Corporate Communication) applicants who do not possess a two-year Master's Degree or two-year Diploma (with minimum 65%) in Mass Communication/Journalism/Advertising & Public Relations specifically - a general MBA or arts degree does not qualify.
  • Jr. Executive (HR) applicants who do not hold a Bachelor's Degree specifically in HR/Personnel/IR/PM & IR with minimum 60% marks - general graduates with HR experience are not eligible.
  • Sr. Manager (IT) applicants who do not hold a full-time regular degree (part-time, distance or correspondence degrees in engineering/MCA do not qualify).
  • Candidates whose post-qualification experience is below the minimum years required for the respective post.
  • Candidates seeking permanent employment or PSU absorption - the contract explicitly states no right of extension or absorption in the company.
  • Government/PSU employees who cannot obtain an NOC from their employer - NOC is mandatory at the time of interview and candidature will be cancelled without it.
  • Executive (Corporate Communication) candidates who have already applied against previous advertisement AWEIL/02/2025 - the notification explicitly advises such candidates not to apply again.
  • Candidates who cannot submit hard copy applications via Speed Post/Courier - online or email applications are not accepted under any circumstances.

Common Mistakes / What to Check Before Applying AWEIL Recruitment 2026?

  • Not writing the post name clearly on the envelope as specified - e.g. "APPLICATION FOR THE POST LEGAL EXPERT" must appear on the envelope; wrong or missing post name can cause misdirection or rejection.
  • Pasting both passport photographs instead of pasting one (4.4 cm x 5.8 cm) on the application form and only attaching (not pasting) the second one - the notification specifically differentiates between the two.
  • Not writing the full name on the back of both passport photographs for identification purposes.
  • Name or spelling mismatch between the application/bio-data and educational/professional qualification certificates - this leads to outright cancellation.
  • Using an email ID or mobile number that may become inactive before the interview date - all communications including interview call will be sent to these details only.
  • OBC candidates applying for Jr. Executive (HR) without a valid, current non-creamy layer OBC certificate - category cannot be changed later.
  • Not filling 'N/A' in columns that are not applicable - leaving columns blank leads to incomplete application rejection.
  • Sending applications by ordinary post or hand delivery instead of Speed Post or Courier - AWEIL takes no responsibility for delay or loss in transit.
  • Govt./PSU employees routing applications directly instead of through proper channel - such applications are summarily rejected.
  • Applying for a post where you meet partial experience but not the specific domain - e.g. general legal experience without corporate/PSU/Govt. sector background for Legal Expert, or general translation experience outside Govt./PSU/University institutions for Official Language Executive.

How to Apply for AWEIL Recruitment 2026

Candidates must apply in Hard Copy only using the prescribed application format for the respective post. The application form can be downloaded from the official AWEIL website (www.aweil.in) or the DoO(C&S) website (ddpdoo.gov.in). Completed applications must be sent via Speed Post or Courier only - no other mode is accepted.

Send Application To:

Director (HR), Advanced Weapons and Equipment India Limited, Ordnance Factory Kanpur (OFC) Premises, Kalpi Road, Kanpur, Uttar Pradesh – 208009

Documents to be Submitted Along with Application:

  • Completed application form in prescribed format with passport photograph (4.4 cm x 5.8 cm) affixed at designated space and second photograph attached (not pasted)
  • Self-attested copy of age proof - Birth Certificate or 10th Class Certificate
  • Self-attested copies of all educational qualification certificates and mark sheets (Graduation level onwards)
  • Self-attested copies of all experience certificates and documentary proof of experience
  • Self-attested copy of Bar Council Registration Certificate (for Legal Expert post)
  • Self-attested copy of caste certificate in prescribed format (for OBC candidates applying for Jr. Executive HR)
  • Aadhar Card Number and PAN Number details (mandatory in application form)
  • No Objection Certificate from current employer (for Govt./PSU employees - mandatory at interview stage)

Documents to be Produced at the Time of Interview:

  • All original documents of educational qualifications and experience for verification
  • Two self-attested copies of all documents
  • Original NOC from employer (for Govt./PSU/Quasi-Govt. employees)
  • For candidates from Govt./PSU/private sector who are finally selected - document evidencing acceptance of resignation from previous employer (mandatory before joining)

Important Instructions for AWEIL Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • The appointment is purely temporary and on contract basis - it does not confer any right or claim of extension or absorption in AWEIL.
  • The contract can be terminated by AWEIL by giving 1 month's notice or payment of 1 month's salary in lieu thereof. Candidates wishing to leave must give 3 months' notice or pay 3 months' salary in lieu.
  • Selected candidates will be required to sign a contractual agreement in the prescribed format.
  • Candidates currently serving in Govt./Quasi-Govt./PSU must seek prior NOC from their employer before applying and must submit it at the time of interview - failure to do so will result in cancellation of candidature.
  • Candidates from Govt./PSU/private entities who are finally selected must produce the acceptance of their resignation from their last employer before joining AWEIL.
  • For candidates selected from the private sector, appointment is effective only after verification of Character and Antecedents from the concerned District Authority (applicable for Executive Corporate Communication post).
  • Any misrepresentation, suppression of facts, use of fraudulent documents or impersonation at any stage will result in permanent debarment and/or legal/criminal prosecution, and dismissal if discovered after joining.
  • AWEIL reserves the right to reject any application or candidature at any stage without assigning any reason. Decision of AWEIL in all matters related to recruitment shall be final and binding.
  • Applications not in prescribed format, incomplete, without required attested certificates, received after 15-06-2026, or not routed through the present employer (for Govt./PSU employees) will be summarily rejected.
  • New Labour Codes will be applicable to all newly engaged contract employees.
  • AWEIL will not be responsible for any delay in receipt or loss of applications in postal transit.
  • No correspondence will be entertained regarding shortlisting, interview schedule or selection decisions.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

Bharat Sanchar Nigam Limited (BSNL), a Government of India Enterprise, has released an official advertisement for 100 posts of Junior Telecom Officer – Direct Recruitment (Telecom) [JTO(T)-DR] from the open market.

FOLLOW US

This recruitment is under Advt. No. BSNLCO-11/12(11)/1/2026-RECTT-CO dated May 26, 2026. Both internal and external candidates fulfilling the prescribed eligibility conditions are eligible to apply. The deadline for online registration is July 3, 2026 at 10:00 AM.

BSNL JTO Recruitment 2026 Key Dates

Essential administrative details regarding the vacancy package include:

Parameter Details
Post Name Junior Telecom Officer – Direct Recruitment (Telecom) [JTO(T)-DR]
Total Vacancies 100
Pay Scale IDA Pay Scale [E-1] of ₹16,400 – ₹40,500/- (2nd PRC pay scale w.e.f. 01.01.2007)
Probation Period Two Years
Bond ₹6 Lakhs bond to serve BSNL for a minimum of 5 years from date of appointment

Eligibility Criteria & Job Requirements

Candidates must fulfill the minimum qualifications specified for the Telecom stream:

Educational Qualification

  • Core Qualification (Telecom Stream): Bachelor of Engineering Degree or equivalent from a Central Govt./State Govt. recognized Institution/University in any of the following disciplines: Telecommunications, Electronics, Radio, Computer, Electrical, Information Technology (IT), or Instrumentation.
  • Alternative Qualification: M.Sc. (Electronics) or M.Sc. (Computer Science).
  • Higher Qualifications: Candidates possessing M.Tech. in the prescribed disciplines are also eligible to appear in the Direct Recruitment examination.
  • Vision Requirement: The applicant must be capable of distinguishing principal colours and should have binocular vision.
  • Nationality: Only Indian Nationals are eligible to apply.

Age Limit & Category-wise Relaxation

General Age Limit: Minimum 20 years and maximum 30 years as on the closing date of receipt of application.

Application Fee

Fee must be paid through online mode only (Internet Banking, Credit Card, Debit Card) on the BSNL website: www.externalexam.bsnl.co.in. Fee once paid is non-refundable.

BSNL JTO Recruitment 2026 Selection Methodology

The selection process is conducted through a Computer Based Multiple Choice Objective Type Test (CBT) as follows:

  1. Written Examination (CBT): 3 hours duration, comprising 120 questions for a total of 480 marks across three sections.
Section Particulars Questions Maximum Marks
Section I Engineering Stream Section – I 50 Questions 200 Marks
Section II Engineering Stream Section – II 50 Questions 200 Marks
Section III General Ability Test Section – III 20 Questions 80 Marks
Total 120 Questions 480 Marks
  1. Marking Scheme: +4 marks for each correct answer and -1 mark for each wrong answer (negative marking applicable).
  2. Minimum Qualifying Marks: Minimum 40% in each section individually. Overall minimum qualifying marks in all three sections combined: 50% for Unreserved/OBC (CL) candidates; 45% for SC/ST/OBC (NCL)/PwBD/EWS candidates.
  3. Document Verification: Selected candidates in the declared merit list will be called for document verification. Date and venue will be intimated in due course.
  4. Medical Examination: Appointment of selected candidates will be subject to passing BSNL's Medical Examination as per standards laid down by BSNL.

How to Apply for BSNL JTO Recruitment 2026

Eligible candidates must complete the online registration process as follows:

  1. Visit the BSNL official examination website: www.externalexam.bsnl.co.in and register online only. No manual/paper applications will be entertained.
  2. Keep the following ready before registering: valid mobile number and email ID (for OTP), scanned passport size colour photograph (jpg/jpeg, 20–50 kb, white background), scanned signature (jpg/jpeg, 10–20 kb), Class 10th Certificate (date of birth proof), Degree Certificate and consolidated marksheet, photo identity proof (Aadhaar/Passport/Driving License/Voter ID), valid category/reservation certificate as applicable, and Medical Certificate for PwBD candidates.
  3. Pay the requisite non-refundable examination fee online through Internet Banking, Credit Card, or Debit Card.
  4. After completing registration, take a printout of the computer-generated registration form and retain it for future reference.
  5. An editing window will be open for 7 days after the registration period closes (July 4–11, 2026). Selective fields can be edited during this period only.
  6. Candidates currently employed in Government Departments/Public Sector Undertakings/Autonomous Bodies must produce a No Objection Certificate (NOC) from their present employer at the time of document verification.
  7. Candidates must select up to 21 Circle preferences (in order of preference) from the registration portal dropdown menu. Options once exercised shall be final.
  8. Admit cards can be downloaded from the BSNL website using the registered username and password well before the examination date.

Engineers India Limited Special Recruitment Drive 2026 Notification PDF

Candidates are strongly advised to read the full notification carefully and choose the position appropriately based on their qualification and experience before applying online.

How to Apply for EIL Special Recruitment Drive 2026

Applications must be submitted online only through the career link on EIL's official website http://www.engineersindia.com. Online submission will be permitted between 0000 hrs on 09.05.2026 till 2359 hrs on 01.06.2026. A candidate can apply for one position only — in case of multiple applications, the latest one shall be considered as final and older applications shall be rejected without notice. Applications once submitted cannot be withdrawn.

Any change in advertisement (including extension of last date) shall be published on EIL's website only — no separate notification shall be issued in press/other medium. The email address specified in the application should be valid/functional for at least 24 months from the date of submission.

Documents to be Scanned and Uploaded at the Time of Online Application:

  • Latest passport size colour photograph in .jpg/.jpeg format (not more than 75 KB)
  • Signature in .jpg/.jpeg format (not more than 25 KB)
  • Date of birth proof — Class X Certificate/Marksheet in .jpg/.jpeg format
  • All experience certificates
  • Certificate/valid documentary proof regarding pay scale (as per Note 3/Note 4 on page 11 of notification) uploaded along with experience documents
  • Disability certificate (PwD) in .jpg/.jpeg/.pdf format (not more than 500 KB)
  • Caste certificate — OBC/SC/ST/EWS certificate, if applicable, in .jpg/.jpeg/.pdf format (not more than 500 KB)

Note: OBC-NCL certificate must be valid as on the closing date of application (25.05.2026). Any candidate applying without uploading valid OBC-NCL certificate will be rejected. Only castes included in the Central list of OBC as given in the official website of National Commission for Backward Classes will be considered.

Documents to be Produced at the Time of Interview/Skill Test (on being called):

  • Proof of Age — Certificate for passing Matriculation/Higher Secondary mentioning date of birth
  • Educational qualifications — all year/semester marksheets and certificates
  • Proof of Experience — Service Certificate of past employment indicating designation, date of joining and release [From-To] & continuity in present organization
  • Pay slips/Salary certificate (duly authenticated) for the past 3 months
  • Certificate/valid documentary proof regarding pay scale (as per Note 3/Note 4)
  • PwD Certificate with photograph (40% or more permanent disability, as per format prescribed by Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment)
  • SC/ST/OBC (NCL)/EWS certificate from the Competent Authority (if applicable)
  • Aadhaar Card
  • Apprenticeship certificate (NATS/NAPS) if applicable
  • NOC from present employer (for candidates working in PSU/Govt. Sector/Govt. Institution)
  • Printout of online application form

Apply Online at: http://www.engineersindia.com (Career Section)

Important Instructions for EIL Special Recruitment Drive 2026 Candidates

  • This is a Special Recruitment Drive for SC, ST & OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) candidates only.
  • Only Full Time Regular courses will be considered (except CA/CMA/CS). The specified qualification should have been acquired within the prescribed minimum course duration.
  • The cut-off date for qualification, experience, and upper age is 30.04.2026.
  • A candidate can apply for one position only. Multiple applications will result in the latest being retained; older applications will be rejected without notice.
  • Applications once submitted cannot be withdrawn. No cancellation/withdrawal requests shall be entertained.
  • OBC-NCL certificate must be in Central format and valid as on the closing date (25.05.2026). Applications without valid OBC-NCL certificate will be rejected.
  • Candidates working in PSU/Govt. Sector must forward their application through proper channel or submit NOC from present employer at the time of interview/skill test. Failure to do so will result in cancellation of candidature and no TA shall be paid.
  • Candidates from PSUs/Govt. Sector must have completed minimum 1 year of service in the immediate lower pay scale as on date of submission of application.
  • Applicant should essentially have proficiency in computers.
  • Failure to submit supporting work experience documents will render claim of experience invalid and make the candidate ineligible from further consideration.
  • PwD candidates — persons with 40% or more permanent nature of disability only are eligible to apply under PwD category.
  • Furnishing of wrong/false information will be a disqualification and EIL will not be responsible for any consequences thereof.
  • EIL reserves the right to cancel the advertisement and/or the selection process without assigning any reason.
  • Court of jurisdiction for any dispute will be at Delhi.
  • Candidates are advised to visit https://www.engineersindia.com regularly for all updates regarding the recruitment process.

How to Apply for Sarkari Job Online 2026

  1. Find the right Sarkari Job: Use the vacancy table or sector links above on OnlyJobs. Filter by your qualification — 10th, 12th, Graduate, or Engineering. Check the last date and eligibility carefully.

  2. Read the official notification: Click on the job listing to open the official notification PDF. Read all eligibility conditions — age limit, educational qualification, experience, category relaxations.

  3. Visit the official recruitment website: Never apply through third-party sites. Use the direct official link provided on OnlyJobs. Look for "Apply Online" or "Register" on the official portal.

  4. Register / Create account: Sign up using your valid email ID and active mobile number. Keep your Aadhaar, bank account details, and email accessible.

  5. Fill the application form: Enter personal details, educational qualifications, category (General/OBC/SC/ST/EWS), address, and other required information accurately.

  6. Upload documents: Scan and upload recent passport-size photograph, signature, 10th certificate (for DOB proof), educational certificates, caste certificate (if applicable). Check file size and format (JPG/PDF as specified).

  7. Pay application fee: Pay via Net Banking, UPI, Debit/Credit Card as applicable. Some categories (SC/ST/PwD/Women) may be exempted from fee. Keep the payment receipt.

  8. Submit and save confirmation: Review all details before final submission. After submitting, download the application printout / PDF confirmation with your registration number. Store it safely for admit card, exam, and future reference.

Why Sarkari Job? Benefits of Government Employment in India

Job Security
Permanent employment — cannot be terminated without due process. Lifelong career stability.
7th Pay Commission Salary
Fixed salary + DA, HRA, TA allowances. DA revised every 6 months. Regular pay revisions.
Medical Benefits
Free or subsidised medical treatment for employee and family under CGHS / state health schemes.
Housing Benefits
Government quarters (where available) or HRA (8–27% of basic pay). No private rent worries.
Pension (NPS / OPS)
Post-retirement monthly pension under NPS or OPS (state-specific). Financial security after service.
Work-Life Balance
Fixed working hours. Paid casual, medical, earned leave. National and state holidays. No overtime culture.
Career Growth
Regular promotions through DPC, ACR-based upgrades, MACP benefits (3 financial upgrades guaranteed).
Children's Education Allowance
Reimbursement of children's school fees (₹2,812/month per child up to 2 children, revised periodically).

Frequently Asked Questions – Sarkari Job 2026

What is Sarkari Job (सरकारी नौकरी)?

Sarkari Job (सरकारी नौकरी) refers to government employment in India — jobs offered by central government departments, state governments, public sector undertakings (PSUs), banks, railways, defence forces, and other government-funded organisations. These jobs offer permanent employment, fixed salary as per 7th Pay Commission, pension, medical benefits, and unmatched job security.

How to find Sarkari Job vacancy 2026 online?

To find online Sarkari Job vacancies in 2026: (1) Visit OnlyJobs daily for updated notifications. (2) Filter by your qualification — 10th, 12th, Graduate, PG. (3) Filter by sector — Railway, Banking, SSC, Defence, State PSC, Teaching. (4) Check last dates carefully. (5) Apply through the official department website using the direct link provided. Enable push notifications on OnlyJobs for instant alerts.

What Sarkari Job can I get after 10th pass?

After 10th pass, you can apply for: Railway Group D (RRB), Indian Army Soldier posts, Postal Mail Guard, SSC MTS / Havaldar, Constable GD, Anganwadi Worker/Helper, ITI Apprentice in PSUs (IOCL, HPCL, NTPC), MCL / Coal India posts, and various state government peon/chowkidar posts. Thousands of 10th pass Sarkari Job vacancies open every year.

What is the salary in Sarkari Job 2026?

Sarkari Job salary in 2026 follows the 7th Pay Commission: Group D / MTS: ₹22,000–₹26,000/month. Clerk / Group C: ₹32,000–₹48,000/month. Officer Grade (SSC CGL, Bank PO): ₹47,000–₹85,000/month. IAS/IPS (UPSC): ₹56,100–₹1,44,200 basic pay + DA, HRA, TA, medical and other allowances. State government salaries vary by state pay commission.

Which Bharat Sarkari Job has the most vacancies in 2026?

The largest Bharat Sarkari Job recruitments in 2026 include: Indian Army Agniveer (40,000+ posts), RRB Group D (22,000+ posts), SSC CGL (14,582 posts), SSC MTS (10,000+ posts), UP Police Constable (multiple), Bihar Police (multiple), IBPS Clerk (8,000+ posts), and various state PSC recruitments. Total active sarkari vacancies in India exceed 52,920 as of March 2026.

What is the best website to find Sarkari Job in India?

OnlyJobs is India's most trusted portal for Sarkari Job notifications since 2011, serving over 10 million users. Official direct sources include: ssc.nic.in (SSC), indianrailways.gov.in (Railways), upsc.gov.in (UPSC), ibps.in (Banking), and respective state PSC websites. OnlyJobs aggregates all official notifications in one place with direct apply links.

How to apply for Sarkari Job online in 2026?

To apply for Sarkari Job online: (1) Read official notification PDF. (2) Check eligibility. (3) Visit official recruitment website. (4) Register with email/mobile. (5) Fill application form. (6) Upload photo, signature, and certificates. (7) Pay fee via Net Banking/UPI/Debit card. (8) Submit and lication confirmation. Save the registration number for admit card and future reference.

What are the benefits of Sarkari Job in India?

Key benefits: (1) Lifelong job security. (2) 7th Pay Commission salary + DA revised biannually. (3) HRA (8–27% of basic). (4) Medical benefits (CGHS). (5) Pension under NPS/OPS. (6) Paid leave — casual, earned, medical. (7) Gratuity after 5 years. (8) Children's education allowance. (9) Fixed working hours and work-life balance. (10) Prestige and social recognition in Indian society.

What is the age limit for Sarkari Job?

Age limits vary by post: Group D (18–25 years), SSC CGL (18–32 years), Bank PO (20–30 years), UPSC Civil Services (21–32 years), Defence Agniveer (17.5–23 years). Age relaxations: SC/ST — 5 years, OBC — 3 years, PwD — 10 years, Ex-Servicemen — 3 years (general), Government servants — up to 5 years. Always verify in the official notification for each post.

What is Bharat Sarkari Job?

Bharat Sarkari Job means government job opportunities across the entire country of India (Bharat) — spanning central and state government vacancies across all 28 states and 8 union territories. The term emphasises pan-India coverage. OnlyJobs covers all Bharat Sarkari Job notifications from UPSC, SSC, Railways, Banking, Defence, state PSCs, and all other government departments.

About this page: Sarkari Job 2026 information on this page is compiled by the OnlyJobs editorial team from official government recruitment notifications. All vacancy data is verified against official websites before listing. OnlyJobs has been India's trusted government job portal since 2011, serving 10+ million users annually. For any recruitment, always refer to the official notification linked above.

About OnlyJobs 2026

OnlyJobs (Government Jobs) are highly sought-after employment opportunities in India. These positions offer job security, good salary packages, allowances, and retirement benefits. Our platform provides comprehensive information about all types of government vacancies including:

  • Central Government Jobs: UPSC, SSC, Railway, Banking, Defence, PSUs
  • State Government Jobs: State PSCs, Police, Teachers, Healthcare workers
  • Qualification-based Jobs: 10th Pass, 12th Pass, Graduate, Post Graduate, Engineering
  • Category-wise Jobs: General, OBC, SC, ST, EWS, Ex-Servicemen, PwD

Frequently Asked Questions - OnlyJobs 2026

Q1: What is the eligibility for OnlyJobs?

Eligibility varies by position. Generally, candidates need to be Indian citizens aged 18-35 years (with age relaxation for reserved categories). Educational qualifications range from 10th pass to post-graduation depending on the job.

Q2: How to prepare for Government Job exams?

Preparation includes understanding the exam pattern, studying from standard books, practicing previous year papers, taking mock tests, and staying updated with current affairs. Focus on subjects like General Knowledge, Reasoning, Quantitative Aptitude, and English.

Q3: Which are the best Government Jobs in 2026?

Top government jobs include IAS/IPS (UPSC), Bank PO, SSC CGL, Railway Group A & B, Defence Services, PSU Jobs, State PCS, and Teaching positions. These offer excellent career growth, job security, and benefits.

Q4: What is the application process for OnlyJobs?

The process typically involves: 1) Online registration on the official website, 2) Filling application form, 3) Uploading documents (photo, signature, certificates), 4) Payment of application fee, 5) Downloading admit card, 6) Appearing for examination, 7) Document verification if selected.

Q5: How often are new OnlyJobs notifications released?

New government job notifications are released daily. Major recruitments like UPSC, SSC, IBPS have annual calendars, while state governments and PSUs release notifications throughout the year. We update our portal daily with 958+ active job listings.

Selection Process for Anganwadi Recruitment 2026

There is no written examination for Anganwadi Worker and Helper posts. Selection is merit-based on academic qualifications. For Supervisor and CDPO posts, a written exam or interview may be conducted.

How to Apply for Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 Online

Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 – FAQ's

How to apply for Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 online?

Visit your state's official WCD / ICDS portal (link provided in table above). Register with mobile & Aadhaar, fill the application form, upload documents (photo, 10th certificate, Aadhaar, domicile), and submit before the last date. Print the application acknowledgement slip after submission.

What is the eligibility for Anganwadi Worker 2026?

Candidates must have passed 10th or 12th standard from a recognized board. Age limit is 18–35 years (varies by state). Must be a permanent resident of the ward/village for which vacancy exists. Only female candidates are eligible for Worker and Helper posts.

What is the salary of Anganwadi Worker in 2026?

Anganwadi Worker receives a monthly honorarium of ₹10,000–₹20,000 depending on the state (Karnataka and TN are highest at ~₹14,000). Anganwadi Helper gets ₹5,000–₹7,000/month. The Central Government pays ₹4,500/month as base honorarium, and states add their own component on top.

Is there a written exam for Anganwadi jobs?

No, there is no written examination for Anganwadi Worker and Anganwadi Helper posts. Selection is purely merit-based on academic qualification marks (10th / 12th percentage). For Supervisor / CDPO posts, a written exam or interview may be conducted depending on the state.

What is the age limit for Anganwadi Helper?

Age limit is 18 to 35 years as of 1st January 2026 in most states. SC/ST candidates get 5 years relaxation (maximum age 40). OBC candidates get 3 years relaxation (maximum age 38). PwD candidates get additional relaxation as per state rules.

What documents are required for Anganwadi job application?

Required documents: Aadhaar card, 10th/12th marksheet and certificate, domicile/residence certificate, caste certificate (SC/ST/OBC), passport-size photograph, scanned signature, and EWS/PwD certificate if applicable. Graduation certificate is needed for Supervisor post applications.

What is ICDS recruitment 2026?

ICDS stands for Integrated Child Development Services - the government programme under which Anganwadi centres operate nationwide. ICDS recruitment 2026 refers to hiring of Anganwadi Workers, Helpers, Supervisors, and CDPOs by state WCD departments under the Ministry of Women & Child Development (wcd.nic.in).

Is Anganwadi Worker a permanent government job?

Anganwadi Workers and Helpers are honorary workers, not regular government employees. They receive a fixed monthly honorarium (not salary) and are entitled to benefits like ESI, health insurance, and maternity benefits. Supervisors and CDPOs are regular government employees in most states and receive full pay & grade pay.

Can I apply for Anganwadi job from a different district?

No. Anganwadi Workers and Helpers must be permanent residents of the same ward/village/district where the vacancy exists. A domicile or residence certificate is mandatory. Applications from outside the specified locality are rejected. For Supervisor/CDPO posts, the same district residency rule generally applies.

Forest Department Posts List

Forest departments work with a clear hierarchy from senior Indian Forest Service officers to field-level staff like Forest Guard and Forest Watcher. Understanding these posts helps candidates know growth opportunities and career progression in forest services.

  • Principal Chief Conservator of Forests (PCCF) / Head of Forest Force
  • Additional Principal Chief Conservator of Forests (APCCF) / Additional Chief Conservator of Forests
  • Chief Conservator of Forests (CCF)
  • Conservator of Forests (CF)
  • Deputy Conservator of Forests (DCF) / Divisional Forest Officer (DFO)
  • Assistant Conservator of Forests (ACF)
  • Forest Range Officer (FRO) / Range Forest Officer (RFO)
  • Deputy Forest Ranger / Forest Inspector
  • Section Forest Officer / Forester / Van Daroga
  • Beat Forest Officer / Beat Guard / Forester Grade I
  • Forest Guard / Van Rakshak
  • Forest Watcher / Forest Worker / Plantation Worker
  • Forest Ranger (in states where used separately)
  • Field Guard

Most forest department recruitment notifications on this page focus on Forest Guard, Forester, Forest Range Officer, Field Guard and similar Group B, C and D posts.

Eligibility for Forest Department Jobs

Eligibility for forest department jobs depends on the post, state and recruitment authority. Below is a general idea of qualification and age for popular posts like Forest Guard, Forester and Forest Range Officer. Candidates should always check the official notification for detailed eligibility criteria.

Education Qualification for Forest Guard, Forester, Ranger

  • Forest Guard / Field Guard / Forest Watcher / Beat Guard: Usually 10th pass or 12th pass from a recognized board, knowledge of local language, and basic physical standards as per state rules.
  • Forester / Section Forest Officer / Deputy Forest Ranger: Generally 12th pass or graduate in any stream or science-related subjects, with physical standards and sometimes driving or technical skills depending on the post.
  • Forest Range Officer (RFO) / Forest Ranger / ACF: Normally graduate degree in Science, Agriculture, Forestry, Environmental Science, Engineering or related disciplines, with higher physical standards and sometimes a competitive exam similar to state PCS level.
  • IFS / Indian Forest Service and senior posts (DCF, CF, CCF, PCCF): Filled through UPSC Indian Forest Service Examination and promotions, requiring a graduate degree and all-India competitive selection.

Age Limit & Relaxation

  • Forest Guard / Forester / Field Guard: Minimum age is usually 18 years and maximum age is around 27–30 years, with age relaxation for SC, ST, OBC, EWS, Ex-servicemen and other categories as per government rules.
  • Forest Range Officer / ACF and higher competitive posts: Age limit often ranges from 21 to 32 years (varies by recruitment), with relaxations for reserved categories.
  • Exact age limit, cut-off date and relaxation details always depend on the particular notification, so candidates should verify from the official advertisement before applying.

Forest Department Jobs Salary Overview

Forest department salary depends on the post, pay level, state and allowances. Senior officers under Indian Forest Service receive higher pay levels, while Forest Guard, Forester and similar posts fall under state government pay matrices.

Selection Process & Exam Pattern

The selection process for Forest Department Jobs 2026 typically includes written examinations, physical tests and document verification. Some posts also conduct medical examination and walking tests due to the demanding nature of field duties.

Written Exam, PET/PST & Document Verification

  • Written Exam: Most Forest Guard, Forester and Forest Range Officer recruitments start with a written exam based on objective-type questions (MCQs).
  • PET / PST (Physical Efficiency Test / Physical Standard Test): Candidates who qualify written exam are called for running, walking test, height, chest measurement and other physical parameters as per post and gender.
  • Document Verification: Shortlisted candidates must produce original certificates of age, qualification, caste, residence, sports/NCC and other documents mentioned in the notification.
  • Medical Examination: Many forest posts require medical fitness to ensure candidates can perform field duties in forests, wildlife areas and difficult terrain.

Syllabus Highlights for Forest Guard & Ranger

Typical syllabus for written exam may include:

  • General Knowledge & Current Affairs (national, state-level, environment and forest-related topics)
  • General Hindi / English or regional language (grammar, comprehension, vocabulary)
  • General Mathematics / Quantitative Aptitude (up to 10th or 12th level)
  • General Science and Environmental Studies (forest, wildlife, biodiversity, conservation, climate, basic biology)
  • Reasoning Ability (logical and analytical reasoning)

Exact subject-wise marks, number of questions, negative marking and exam duration will be given in the respective forest department notification.

How to Apply for Forest Department Jobs 2026

Most Forest Department Jobs 2026 applications are accepted either online through official websites or offline via prescribed application forms. Candidates should carefully read the official notification before filling the form.

Online Application Process

  1. Visit the official website of the recruiting body (State Forest Department, State PSC, Staff Selection Board or other government portal).
  2. Find the latest link for Forest Guard / Forester / Forest Range Officer / other forest posts and open the detailed advertisement.
  3. Complete the registration process with basic details like name, mobile number, email ID and verify using OTP if required.
  4. Fill the online application form with personal, educational, address and category information exactly as per documents.
  5. Upload scanned copies of photograph, signature and other certificates in the prescribed format and size.
  6. Pay the application fee online through Net Banking, Debit Card, Credit Card or UPI as per your convenience.
  7. Finally, submit the form and take a printout or PDF of the submitted application and payment receipt for future reference.

Documents Required & Application Fee

  • Recent passport size photograph and signature (scanned)
  • Class 10th and 12th mark sheets and certificates
  • Degree / Diploma certificates for graduate-level posts
  • Caste / Category certificate (SC, ST, OBC, EWS, etc.)
  • Domicile / Residence certificate if required by the state
  • NCC, Sports, Ex-serviceman and other quota certificates if applicable
  • Valid ID proof (Aadhaar Card, PAN, Voter ID, Driving Licence, Passport, etc.)

Why Choose Forest Department Jobs?

Forest department jobs are popular among candidates who love nature, wildlife and field work. These jobs offer job security, government benefits and a chance to work in forest conservation, wildlife protection, eco-tourism and community development.

  • Stable government job with regular salary, pension/NPS and allowances
  • Opportunity to work in forests, wildlife sanctuaries, national parks and protected areas
  • Contribution to environment protection, afforestation and biodiversity conservation
  • Good promotion avenues from Forest Guard to Forester, Forest Range Officer and higher posts in many states.

Frequently Asked Questions - OnlyJobs 2026

Q1: What is OnlyJobs?

OnlyJobs is a trusted Indian job and exam information portal. Find latest sarkari job vacancies, exam results, admit cards, online forms at OnlyJobs.OnlyJobs.

Q2: How can I check the latest Sarkari jobs at OnlyJobs?

You can check the latest Sarkari jobs on OnlyJobs by visiting the latest jobs page, scanning the newest recruitment listings, and clicking the job title to see full details such as eligibility, application date, and official notification.

Q3: Does OnlyJobs provide सरकारी नौकरी updates?

Yes, OnlyJobs shares daily updates on सरकारी नौकरी, including vacancies from Railway, SSC, Banking, UPSC, Police, Army, and state-level recruitments.

Q4: Can I find admit cards and answer keys on OnlyJobs?

Yes, the website also publishes admit cards, answer keys, syllabus, cut-off marks, and other important exam-related notifications.Result notification service.

Q5: Is OnlyJobs an official government website?

No, OnlyJobs is a private information portal. It collects and publishes recruitment and exam updates from official government sources for public convenience.

Q6: Is this the official OnlyJobs website?

No. OnlyJobs is a private information portal, not a government website. All results, admit cards, and notifications link to their respective official government portals such as ssc.nic.in, upsc.gov.in, rrbcdg.gov.in, and ibps.in. OnlyJobs has been providing this aggregation service since 2011.

Q7: How do I get OnlyJobs alerts on my phone?

Download the OnlyJobs mobile app from Store, join the official WhatsApp Channel, or subscribe to the Telegram group. You will receive instant push notifications whenever a new result, admit card, or recruitment notification is published.

Q8: How can I check my OnlyJobs 2026?

Visit OnlyJobs.OnlyJobs daily. Find your exam in the Latest Results section, click the link, and you will be taken to the official government portal. Enter your Roll Number or Registration ID to view your result.

SSC GD Admit Card 2026 OUT for 4th Phase - Download Hall Ticket at ssc.gov.in

SSC GD Constable 4th Phase Admit Card 2026 has been officially released by the Staff Selection Commission (SSC). Candidates who have applied for the SSC GD Constable Recruitment 2026 can now download their admit card from the official website ssc.gov.in. The Computer-Based Test (CBT) for the 4th Phase is going to be held from 25 May 2026 to 30 May 2026 across various exam centers in India. This hall ticket is compulsory to appear in the examination.

FOLLOW US

SSC GD Admit Card 2026 - Quick Information

  • 4th Phase Exam Dates: 25 May to 30 May 2026
  • Download Status: Active Now
  • Official Website: ssc.gov.in
  • Exam Mode: Computer-Based Test (CBT)
  • Vacancies: 25487 (approx.)

SSC GD Admit Card 2026 - Direct Download Link

Download Admit Card Now

 

SSC GD Admit Card 2026 Overview

Particulars Details
Conducting Body Staff Selection Commission (SSC)
Exam Name SSC GD Constable 2026 (4th Phase)
Admit Card Release April 2026 onwards (Phase-wise)
4th Phase Exam Dates 25 May – 30 May 2026
Exam Mode Computer-Based Test (CBT)
Official Website ssc.gov.in

How to Download SSC GD Admit Card 2026?

  • Visit the official SSC website: ssc.gov.in
  • Click on the “Admit Card” section or login portal
  • Select your Regional SSC Office (e.g., Northern Region, Southern Region etc.)
  • Enter your Application Number / Registration ID and Date of Birth or Password
  • Solve the captcha and click Submit
  • Your SSC GD Admit Card 2026 will appear on the screen
  • Download and take 2-3 printouts for the exam

Pro Tip: Verify all details (name, photo, exam center, date & shift) immediately after downloading. Report discrepancies to SSC helpdesk right away.

SSC GD 2026 - Important Dates

Event Date Status
Application Period Nov–Dec 2025 Completed
City Intimation Slip April 2026 Released
Admit Card Release April 2026 onwards Live Now
4th Phase Exam 25–30 May 2026 Ongoing
Result Declaration To be announced Awaited

Details on SSC GD Admit Card 2026

Your admit card contains:

  • Candidate Information: Name, Roll Number, Photograph, Signature, Category, DOB
  • Exam Details: Exam Date, Shift/Timing, Reporting Time, Exam Center Name & Address, Center Code

Documents Required on SSC GD Exam Day

Mandatory Documents (Originals):

  • Printed SSC GD Admit Card 2026
  • Valid Photo ID Proof (Aadhaar, Voter ID, PAN, Passport, Driving License)
  • Recent passport-size photographs (if specified)
  • Black/Blue Ballpoint Pen

Not Allowed:

  • Mobile phones, smartwatches, calculators
  • Any electronic devices, books, notes, bags

Important Instructions for SSC GD 4th Phase Exam 2026

  • Reach the exam center at least 45-60 minutes before reporting time
  • Entry closes 30 minutes before the exam starts
  • Follow all COVID-19 or other guidelines mentioned on the admit card
  • Carry transparent water bottle and light snacks for outside the hall

What If SSC GD Admit Card Not Available?

  • Clear browser cache/cookies and try again
  • Use a different browser (Chrome recommended)
  • Check your application status
  • Contact your respective SSC Regional Office immediately

📄 Complete Details & Notification

The latest Union Public Service Commission (UPSC) Recruitment 2026 has been announced for various Group A and Group B posts across multiple Central Government Ministries and Departments! Published as Advertisement No. 05/2026 at the official UPSC website, this new recruitment drive offers 194 vacancies across 32 posts in departments including DGCA, PESO, Department of Posts, IMD, Archaeological Survey of India, Ministry of Defence, Ministry of MSME, and more. Eligible Indian citizens with qualifications ranging from Bachelor's degree in Engineering to Master's degree/Ph.D. can apply online from 23-05-2026 to 12-06-2026 (up to 1800 Hrs). Find all details about UPSC Advertisement No. 05/2026 new vacancy 2026, including eligibility, online form, and notification PDF here.

FOLLOW US

Union Public Service Commission (UPSC) Advertisement No. 05/2026 Notification PDF

Union Public Service Commission (UPSC) has officially released Advertisement No. 05/2026 inviting Online Recruitment Applications for direct recruitment by selection to various Group A and Group B Gazetted posts across Central Government Ministries and Departments. The notification includes complete details on post-wise vacancies, reservation position, essential qualifications, experience requirements, age limits, pay scales, duties, and application instructions. Candidates are strongly advised to read the full notification carefully before applying online at https://upsconline.nic.in/ora/.

  • Download UPSC Advertisement No. 05/2026 Official Notification PDF

UPSC Recruitment 2026 Overview

UPSC is recruiting for 32 different posts across Central Government Departments with qualifications ranging from BE/B.Tech to Master's/Ph.D. degree, last date 12 June 2026 (1800 Hrs).

Organization Name Union Public Service Commission (UPSC), New Delhi
Advertisement No. 05/2026
Post Names Airworthiness Officer (DGCA), Deputy Controller of Explosive (PESO), Manager (Mail Motor Service), Junior Technical Officer (Sugar & Veg Oils), Junior Technical Officer (Chemical Engineering), Scientific Officer (Mechanical), Deputy Superintending Archaeological Engineer (ASI), Associate Professor Electrical Engineering (CME Pune), Associate Professor Mechanical Engineering (CME Pune), Lecturer Persian Language (SFL), Lecturer Tibetan Language (SFL), Scientist-B (Atmospheric Sciences / General Meteorology / Instrumentation) (IMD), Deputy Director Civil Engineering (CICEF), Joint Assistant Director (Directorate of Coordination Police Wireless), Assistant Executive Engineer (CGWB), Deputy Director of Mines Safety (Mining), Assistant Director Grade-I/Grade-II (IEDS Chemical) (MSME), and other posts
Total Vacancies 194 posts across 32 vacancies (subject to change as per service requirements)
Pay Scale Level 7 to Level 13A1 as per 7th CPC Pay Matrix (varies by post)
Job Type Permanent Central Government Service - Group A & Group B Gazetted, Non-Ministerial (varies by post)
Application Mode Online only - at https://upsconline.nic.in/ora/
Selection Mode Recruitment by Selection (Interview / Recruitment Test followed by Interview - as decided by UPSC post-wise)
Opening of Online Application 23-05-2026
Last Date for Online Application 12-06-2026 (1800 Hrs)
Official Website https://upsconline.nic.in/ora/ | https://upsc.gov.in

UPSC Recruitment 2026 Important Dates

Event Date
Date of Advertisement 22-05-2026 / 23-05-2026
Opening of Online Recruitment Application 23-05-2026
Last Date for Online Recruitment Application 12-06-2026 (1800 Hrs)
Date for Determining Eligibility 12-06-2026 (Closing date for Online Recruitment Application)
Interview / Personality Test Date To be intimated separately via email and UPSC website

UPSC Recruitment 2026 - Vacancy Details

A total of 32 vacancy notifications have been released under Advertisement No. 05/2026 across various Central Government Ministries and Departments. Vacancies are distributed category-wise (UR/EWS/OBC/SC/ST) with horizontal reservation for PwBD candidates wherever applicable. All vacancies are to be filled as per service requirement. The number of vacancies may change depending on service requirements.

26050502523 Deputy Controller of Explosive PESO, Dept. for Promotion of Industry & Internal Trade, Ministry of Commerce & Industry 44 UR-16, EWS-03, OBC-13, SC-07, ST-05, PwBD-01* Level 10 35 (UR/EWS), 38 (OBC), 40 (SC/ST)

Abbreviations: UR – Unreserved, EWS – Economically Weaker Sections, OBC – Other Backward Classes, SC – Scheduled Castes, ST – Scheduled Tribes, PwBD – Persons with Benchmark Disabilities. * PwBD reservation is horizontal within overall vacancies for that post. PwBD candidates with disability not less than 40% are eligible for reservation/relaxation where posts are reserved for them, and for other relaxations where posts are identified as suitable for them.

Post-wise Essential Qualifications Summary

Post Essential Qualification Essential Experience
Airworthiness Officer (DGCA) Bachelor's degree in Physics/Mathematics/Aircraft Maintenance or Engineering Degree in Aeronautical/Mechanical/Electrical/Electronics/Telecommunication from recognized university; AND valid AME License in Category B1 or B2 issued by DGCA (Aeronautical Engineering degree holders may be considered without AME License) Minimum 3 years of Aircraft Maintenance experience on operating aircraft in DGCA-approved Aircraft Maintenance Organization
Deputy Controller of Explosive (PESO) Degree in Chemical Engineering/Technology OR Master's Degree in Chemistry from recognized University/Institute 3 years' experience in Chemical works or manufacture/handling of explosives and/or compressed gases and/or refining of Petroleum
Manager (Mail Motor Service) BE/B.Tech in Mechanical or Automobile Engineering from recognized University/Institute 3 years' experience in operation of Motor Transport Traffic in a Govt./Semi Govt./PSU/Company listed on Indian stock exchange(s)
Junior Technical Officer (Sugar & Veg Oils) B.Tech/BE (Oil Technology) OR B.Sc. with PG Diploma in Sugar Technology from recognized University/Institute 1 year experience in Sugar Technology or Edible Oils
Junior Technical Officer (Chemical Engineering) - NSI Kanpur Master's Degree in Chemical Engineering from recognized University/Institute 3 years' research/practical/teaching experience in Chemical Engineering from recognized College/University/Autonomous body OR 3 years' experience in Sugar factory/Distillery/Chemical Plant design or operation
Scientific Officer (Mechanical) - NTH Master's Degree in Physics OR Degree in Mechanical Engineering OR Degree in Metallurgy from recognized University/Institute 1 year experience in Mechanical Testing Laboratory OR 1 year experience in Research and Development in allied field
Deputy Superintending Archaeological Engineer (ASI) Bachelor's Degree in Civil Engineering from a recognized University/Institute 3 years' experience in planning, execution and maintenance of civil engineering works in Government Sector or any recognized Archaeological or heritage site
Associate Professor, Electrical Engineering (CME Pune) BE/B.Tech in Electrical Engineering AND ME/M.Tech in Electrical Engineering (Power System or related specializations) with First Class; AND Ph.D. in Electrical Engineering Minimum 5 years' experience in Teaching/Research/Industry, of which 2 years post-Ph.D. experience is desirable
Associate Professor, Mechanical Engineering (CME Pune) BE/B.Tech in Mechanical Engineering AND ME/M.Tech in Mechanical Engineering (Heat Transfer/Thermal Engineering or related specializations) with First Class; AND Ph.D. in Mechanical Engineering Minimum 5 years' experience in Teaching/Research/Industry, of which 2 years post-Ph.D. experience is desirable
Lecturer (Persian Language) - SFL Master's degree in Persian Language from a recognized University/Institution 1 year experience in teaching or translation from Persian to English/Hindi or vice-versa
Lecturer (Tibetan Language) - SFL Master's degree in Tibetan Language from a recognized University/Institution 1 year experience in teaching or translation from Tibetan to English/Hindi or vice-versa
Scientist-B (Atmospheric Sciences) - IMD Master's Degree in Engineering & Technology in Atmospheric Sciences/Ocean Science/Hydrology/Atmospheric Physics/Atmospheric Ocean Science & Technology/Radar & Microwave Engineering from recognized University/Institute Nil (No experience specified)
Scientist-B (General Meteorology) - IMD Master's Degree in Science in Mathematics/Physics/Atmospheric Science or Physics/Meteorology from recognized University/Institute Nil (No experience specified)
Scientist-B (Instrumentation) - IMD Bachelor's Degree in Engineering/Technology in Electronics & Communication/Mechanical/IT/Instrumentation/Mechatronics/Electrical/Computer Science from recognized University/Institute Nil (No experience specified)
Deputy Director (Civil Engineering) - CICEF Bachelor's Degree in Civil Engineering OR Master's Degree in Civil Engineering from recognized University/Institute For B.E./B.Tech holders: 4 years in harbour projects and maritime structures in any Govt. organization. For M.E./M.Tech holders: 2 years in harbour projects and maritime structures
Joint Assistant Director (Police Wireless) B.Tech/BE/B.Sc. (Engnr.) in Electronics/Electronics & Telecom/Electronics & Communication/IT/Computer Science/ICT/Electrical Engineering (with Telecom special subject) OR Master's degree in Electronics/Computer Science/IT/Artificial Intelligence/Physics with Electronics/Communication/Wireless/Radio specialization Nil (Experience is desirable, not essential)
Assistant Executive Engineer - CGWB Bachelor's Degree in Drilling/Mining/Mechanical/Electrical/Civil/Agricultural Engineering or Petroleum Technology from recognized University/Institute 2 years' experience in operation/maintenance/repair of Drilling Rigs or construction of Tube wells or Ground Water Development and Management
Deputy Director of Mines Safety (Mining) Degree in Mining Engineering from recognized University/Institute OR pass in Section A & B of AMIE in Mining Engineering Branch (enrolled up to 31.05.2013 only); AND First Class Mine Manager's Certificate (Unrestricted) under Coal Mines Regulations, 2017 or Metalliferous Mines Regulations, 1961 10 years' experience in mining in large and mechanized underground mines
Assistant Director Grade-I (IEDS) (Chemical) - MSME Master's Degree in Chemistry or Industrial Chemistry OR Bachelor's Degree in Chemical Technology or Chemical Engineering from recognized University/Institute Nil (No experience specified)

UPSC Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the closing date of online application i.e. 12-06-2026 (1800 Hrs). Mere possession of minimum essential qualifications does not entitle candidates to be called for interview - UPSC may shortlist candidates based on desirable qualifications, higher educational qualifications, higher experience, or by holding a Recruitment Test. All candidates in Government service (permanent/temporary, other than casual/adhoc/daily wages) must submit a declaration that they have informed their Head of Office/Department in writing that they have applied.

Criteria Details
Citizenship Citizen of India, or subject of Nepal/Bhutan, or Tibetan refugee (came before 01-01-1962 intending to permanently settle in India), or person of Indian origin migrated from Pakistan/Burma/Sri Lanka/East African countries intending to permanently settle in India - subject to Government of India eligibility certificate where applicable.
Educational Qualification Varies by post - from Bachelor's degree in Engineering to Master's degree/Ph.D. in the relevant discipline. Refer post-wise details in the notification.
Experience Varies by post - from nil to 10 years of relevant experience. Part-time/daily wages/visiting/guest faculty experience will NOT be counted. Refer post-wise details.
OBC - Non Creamy Layer OBC candidates must submit a declaration that they do not belong to the Creamy Layer. The closing date of receipt of Online Recruitment Application is the crucial date for OBC non-creamy layer status.
PwBD Eligibility PwBD candidates with disability not less than 40% are eligible. Those with "likely to improve" (temporary disability) are NOT eligible for reservation. Candidates can apply for suitable posts even if not reserved for PwBD - but will be considered by general merit standard without relaxation.
Government Employees Persons already in regular government service must submit a declaration that they have informed their Head of Office/Department in writing of their application. Casual/adhoc/daily wages/hourly paid/contract employees should apply directly without such declaration.
Equivalent Qualification Candidates claiming equivalent qualifications must produce order/letter indicating the Authority (with number and date) under which it has been treated as equivalent; otherwise the application will be rejected.

UPSC Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit

Age limits are post-specific and are calculated as on the closing date for submission of online recruitment application i.e. 12-06-2026. The age limits shown in the advertisement are the normal limits for UR/EWS candidates. Standard age relaxations are applicable for SC/ST (5 years), OBC (3 years), and PwBD (10 years) for vacancies reserved/suitable for them. OBC/SC/ST/PwBD candidates applying against unreserved posts are not entitled to age relaxation.

Category Age Relaxation
SC / ST 5 years relaxation (for vacancies reserved for them)
OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) 3 years relaxation (for vacancies reserved for them)
PwBD (General) 10 years relaxation (for vacancies reserved/suitable for them)
Ex-Servicemen / Commissioned Officers including ECOs/SSCOs As per prescribed proforma from competent authority - as per Concessions & Relaxations section of UPSC notification
Central / UT Government Employees/Servants As per prescribed proforma from competent authority issued after date of advertisement

UPSC Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

Application fee is to be paid online only and is non-refundable. Fee once paid shall not be refunded under any circumstance nor can it be held in reserve for any other examination or selection. Applications without the prescribed fee will be summarily rejected without any representation being entertained.

Category Application Fee
General / OBC / EWS (Male candidates) Rs. 25/- (Non-Refundable) - via Visa/Master/Rupay Credit/Debit Card/UPI/Internet Banking
SC / ST / PwBD / Women (all communities) NIL (Exempted from fee payment)

UPSC Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

Selection will be made by Recruitment by Selection - through Interview or Recruitment Test followed by Interview as decided by UPSC on a post-by-post basis. In the event of a large number of applications, UPSC will adopt shortlisting criteria including desirable qualifications, higher educational qualifications, higher experience, experience before/after essential qualification, or by conducting a Recruitment Test. The summoning of candidates for interview conveys no assurance of selection. Appointment orders will be issued by the respective Ministry.

Stage 1 - Application Scrutiny and Shortlisting

All applications are scrutinized for eligibility. If applications are large in number, UPSC may shortlist based on: (a) Desirable Qualifications; (b) higher educational qualifications; (c) higher relevant experience; (d) counting experience before/after essential qualification; (e) invoking experience even where not mentioned as EQ/DQ; or (f) by holding a Recruitment Test. Candidates should mention all qualifications and experience in the relevant field over and above the minimum qualifications.

Stage 2 - Recruitment Test (RT) followed by Interview (wherever applicable)

If selection is made by Recruitment Test followed by Interview, candidates must achieve minimum suitability at both stages. Minimum interview marks: UR – 50, OBC – 45, SC/ST/PH – 40 (out of 100 total interview marks). Minimum marks for Recruitment Test shall be decided by UPSC on a case-by-case basis.

Stage 3 - Medical Examination

Selected candidates must be in sound bodily health and must undergo medical examination and satisfy medical authority as required by the Government.

How to Apply for UPSC Recruitment 2026

Candidates must apply online only at https://upsconline.nic.in/ora/. The UPSC online portal has four modules: Account Creation, Universal Registration (URN), Common Application Form (CAF), and Post/Vacancy-specific module. The first three modules can be filled at any time; the fourth (vacancy-specific) module must be filled during the application window. Candidates who have already registered and have a URN can directly log in and fill the vacancy-specific module. Candidates are strongly advised to use Aadhaar Card as the ID document for seamless verification. Applications once submitted are final - no correction/alteration/modification is permitted after submission. Candidates must take a printout of the finally submitted application and carry it along with original documents if called for interview.

Documents to be Uploaded at the time of Online Application:

  • Live photograph captured via webcam/camera during CAF filling (mandatory)
  • Scanned recent passport-size photograph (as per instructions on UPSC website)
  • Scanned signature (three signatures one below the other on plain white paper, black ink)
  • Matriculation/10th Standard certificate or marksheet as proof of Date of Birth
  • Degree/Diploma certificate as proof of educational qualification; if not available, provisional certificate + all year marksheets
  • Experience Certificate(s) in prescribed format from Head(s) of Organisation(s) - mentioning duration, basic pay, consolidated pay, and nature of duties
  • Caste/Community certificate (SC/ST/OBC) in prescribed proforma from Competent Authority
  • OBC Non-Creamy Layer declaration in prescribed format
  • EWS certificate in prescribed proforma
  • PwBD Disability Certificate from Medical Board constituted by Central/State Government
  • Age relaxation certificate for Ex-Servicemen/Central/UT Government Employees in prescribed proforma
  • Order/letter for equivalent qualification (if applicable) citing Authority with number and date
  • Name change documents (if applicable - marriage certificate/divorce decree/affidavit as per situation)
  • Documentary support for any other claim made (Professional Registration, Publications, GATE/NET, etc.)

Printout of the finally submitted Online Recruitment Application must be retained - candidates will need to bring it to the interview along with original certificates. Candidates are NOT required to send printouts or documents by post or by hand to UPSC.

Apply Online at: https://upsconline.nic.in/ora/

For queries: Helpline No. 011-24041001 or 011-40303444 | Email: [email protected] (10:00 AM to 5:30 PM on all working days during the application window).

Important Instructions for UPSC Advertisement No. 05/2026 Candidates

  • Online applications open from 23-05-2026 and close on 12-06-2026 at 1800 Hrs - candidates must apply well in advance without waiting for the closing date.
  • All candidates must enter correct and active email addresses - all correspondence including interview schedules will be communicated only through the registered email ID.
  • Applications once submitted are final - no correction/alteration/modification in any field is allowed after submission. No withdrawal of applications is permitted.
  • The date for determining eligibility in every respect is the closing date for submission of online recruitment application (12-06-2026).
  • Filling wrong information may lead to rejection through computer-based shortlisting apart from debarment by the Commission.
  • Face authentication will be mandatory for all candidates at the examination venue/Interview Hall - candidates must enter well in time for face authentication and frisking.
  • Mobile phones (even in switched-off mode), pagers, smart watches, pen drives, cameras, Bluetooth devices, or any electronic/communication device are strictly banned at the examination/interview venue. Violation leads to disciplinary action including ban from future examinations/recruitment.
  • Candidates must not bring valuables or bags to the venue - no arrangement for safe-keeping will be made and UPSC will not be responsible for any loss.
  • The Commission will contribute towards interview travelling expenses at Second Class Mail railway fare rate by shortest route - candidates will be informed of details when called for interview.
  • Canvassing in any form will disqualify a candidate.
  • Summoning for interview conveys no assurance of selection - appointment orders will be issued by the respective Ministry.
  • UPSC may grant higher initial pay to candidates adjudged meritorious in the interview.
  • The Commission does not enter into correspondence with candidates about reasons for non-selection - no interim enquiry about results will be entertained.
  • Prescribed proforma for various certificates are available at https://upsc.gov.in under Recruitment → Forms of Certificates.

EdCIL (India) Ltd. (A Mini Ratna Category-I CPSE, Government of India) has published the Multidisciplinary Staff Notification 2026 on 22 May 2026. The recruitment drive aims to fill 879 Multidisciplinary Staff and PMU Consultant positions. Candidates within the age range of up to 45 years and holding the prescribed qualifications from recognized institutions are eligible to apply online at the official EdCIL recruitment portal. The application process will be active from 22 May 2026 to 04 June 2026, and candidates can easily access the notification PDF and apply link below.

FOLLOW US

EdCIL Multidisciplinary Staff 2026 Notification PDF

EdCIL (India) Ltd has published the official Recruitment Notification for engagement of Multidisciplinary Staff and PMU Consultants for Autism Centres in Andhra Pradesh under Inclusive Education–PM SHRI. The notification carries full details of post-wise vacancies, remuneration, educational qualifications, experience, age limits, selection process, general instructions, application procedure, and the online application link. Candidates are advised to carefully read the complete advertisement before submitting their online applications.

  • Download EdCIL Multidisciplinary Staff 2026 Notification PDF

EdCIL Multidisciplinary Staff 2026 Overview

Organization Name EdCIL (India) Ltd. (A Mini Ratna Category-I CPSE, Government of India)
Advertisement No. TSG/Autism/Multidisciplinary-Team/2026-01
Project / Scheme Inclusive Education – PM SHRI, Andhra Pradesh (Autism Centres)

EdCIL Multidisciplinary Staff 2026 Important Dates

Event Date
Date of Advertisement 22 May 2026

EdCIL Multidisciplinary Staff 2026 – Post-wise Vacancy & Remuneration

EdCIL is hiring for 879 Multidisciplinary Staff including Psychologist, Speech Therapist, Clinical Psychologist, PMU Consultant and Occupational Therapist, last date 4 June 2026.

S.No. Post Name No. of Posts Remuneration (per month)
1 Occupational Therapist 125 Rs. 30,000/-
2 Physiotherapist 125 Rs. 30,000/-
3 Psychologist 125 Rs. 30,000/-
4 Speech Therapist 125 Rs. 30,000/-
5 Clinical Psychologist 125 Rs. 30,000/-
6 Special Educator (Secondary Level) 125 Rs. 30,000/-
7 Special Educator (Primary Level) 125 Rs. 25,000/-
8 PMU Consultant 4 Rs. 55,000/-

EdCIL Multidisciplinary Staff 2026 – Educational Qualifications

Candidates must possess the prescribed qualifications from Government/RCI recognized institutions and fulfil the eligibility criteria as on 30 April 2026.

Occupational Therapist (125 Posts)

  • Bachelor’s or Master’s Degree in Occupational Therapy from a recognized institution.
  • Valid registration/license to practise as an Occupational Therapist in India, as per applicable state regulations.
  • Strong clinical reasoning and problem-solving abilities, excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
  • Ability to work independently and collaboratively within a team, with a compassionate, patient-centred approach.
  • Experienced candidates in the relevant field will be given added advantage.

Physiotherapist (125 Posts)

  • Bachelor’s / Master’s Degree in Physiotherapy from a recognized institution.
  • Valid professional registration/license.
  • Strong clinical reasoning and communication skills, compassionate approach, ability to work independently and in a team.
  • Experienced candidates in the relevant field will be given added advantage.

Psychologist (125 Posts)

  • M.A./M.Sc in Psychology from a recognized university (Clinical/Counselling/Developmental Psychology preferred).
  • Experience in psychological assessments and behavioural therapies.
  • Strong communication, counselling and interpersonal skills, ability to work independently and as part of a team.
  • Experienced candidates in the relevant field will be given added advantage.

Speech Therapist (125 Posts)

  • Bachelor’s / Master’s Degree in Speech-Language Pathology or Audiology & Speech-Language Pathology from a recognized institution.
  • Mandatory valid registration with the Rehabilitation Council of India (RCI).
  • Experience working with children with Autism and developmental disorders preferred.
  • Strong assessment, communication and interpersonal skills.
  • Proficiency in Telugu and English (knowledge of other local languages is an added advantage).
  • Ability to work independently and as part of a team; experienced candidates will be given added advantage.

Clinical Psychologist (125 Posts)

  • Post Graduation in Clinical Psychology from an institution recognized by the Rehabilitation Council of India (RCI).
  • Mandatory valid RCI registration.
  • Strong expertise in psychological assessments and behavioural therapies.
  • Excellent communication and interpersonal skills; proficiency in Telugu is an added advantage.
  • Ability to work independently and as part of a multidisciplinary team; experienced candidates will be given added advantage.

Special Educator (Secondary Level) – 125 Posts

  • The candidate must possess any one of the following RCI-recognised qualifications with valid CRR number:
    • Bachelor’s Degree + B.Ed. (Special Education), OR
    • Bachelor’s Degree + B.Ed. (General) with 1-year Diploma in Autism/Intellectual Disability, OR
    • Bachelor’s Degree + B.Ed. (General) with D.Ed. in Autism/Intellectual Disability.
  • Qualified AP TET (Paper-2B mandatory) / CTET in Special Education.
  • Experienced candidates in the relevant field will be given added advantage.

Special Educator (Primary Level) – 125 Posts

  • Must have passed Intermediate (10+2) from a recognized Board.
  • Must possess a two-year Diploma in Special Education (Autism/Intellectual Disability) recognized by RCI with valid CRR number.
  • Qualified AP TET (Paper-1B mandatory) / CTET in Special Education.
  • Experienced candidates in the relevant field will be given added advantage.

PMU Consultant (4 Posts)

  • Ph.D./M.Phil in Clinical Psychology/Psychology/Special Education.
  • Desirable: Ph.D in the field of rehabilitation of Persons with Disabilities.
  • Experienced candidates in the relevant field will be given added advantage.
  • Expertise in report writing and strong communication skills is mandatory.

EdCIL Multidisciplinary Staff 2026 - Age Limit

S.No. Post Name Upper Age Limit
1 Occupational Therapist 45 years
2 Physiotherapist 45 years
3 Psychologist 45 years
4 Speech Therapist 45 years
5 Clinical Psychologist 45 years
6 Special Educator (Secondary Level) 45 years
7 Special Educator (Primary Level) 45 years
8 PMU Consultant 45 years

EdCIL Multidisciplinary Staff 2026 – General Instructions

  • Only online applications will be accepted; only Indian Nationals are eligible.
  • The workplace will be Autism Centres across various Municipalities of Andhra Pradesh as approved by SPD (Inclusive Education–PM SHRI, Andhra Pradesh).
  • The competent authority reserves the right to amend, cancel or change the advertisement wholly or partly without assigning any reason.
  • The engagement will not confer any right for regular engagement, permanent absorption or continued contractual engagement.
  • Qualifications must be from Government recognized institutions only.
  • Candidates should have strong verbal and written communication skills and excellent working knowledge of computers (MS Word, MS Excel, MS PowerPoint etc.) and presentation skills.
  • Selection will be based on academic/professional qualifications and may include an interview on a prescribed date and time as decided by the competent authority.
  • A waiting list of candidates will be prepared for possible future vacancies or non-joining of selected candidates.
  • Original documents to prove qualifications, age, experience, etc., must be produced at the time of interview.
  • The cut-off date for age and experience is 30 April 2026; eligibility must be satisfied as on this date.
  • Candidates must upload all required documents (educational certificates, resume, experience certificates etc.) in PDF format only; failure will result in disqualification/rejection.
  • Screening and shortlisting will strictly be based on details and documents provided in the online application; incomplete, false or misrepresented information will lead to disqualification.
  • Any false declaration or suppression of information detected at any stage will lead to cancellation of candidature/termination of services and may attract disciplinary/legal action.
  • Interview letters and related communication will be sent only to shortlisted candidates via e-mail as provided in the online application; candidates must keep their email active.
  • Merely fulfilling eligibility criteria does not entitle a candidate to be called for interview.
  • Engagement is purely contractual; extension of contract depends on the needs of Inclusive Education–PM SHRI, Andhra Pradesh and the performance of the professional.
  • The last date for receipt of online applications is 04 June 2026.
  • No application fee is required for any of the posts.

How to Apply for EdCIL Multidisciplinary Staff 2026

Candidates must apply online only; no other mode of application will be accepted.

  1. Visit the online recruitment portal at https://tsgrecruitment.edcil.co.in/login.
  2. Register and fill in the online application form with accurate and complete details of personal information, qualifications and experience.
  3. Upload all required documents such as educational certificates, experience certificates, resume and other supporting documents in PDF format only.
  4. Verify all details before final submission, as screening and shortlisting will be based only on the information and documents submitted.
  5. Submit the online application on or before 04 June 2026.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

The latest Punjab & Sind Bank (PSB) Recruitment 2026 has been announced for Apprentice posts! Published on 21-05-2026 at the official website of Punjab & Sind Bank, this new recruitment drive offers 635 openings for eligible candidates. Applicants within the 20 to 28 years age group and possessing a Regular Graduation degree from a recognized University/Institute can apply online from 21-05-2026 to 06-06-2026. Find all details about the Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice new vacancy 2026, including eligibility, online form, and notification PDF here.

FOLLOW US

Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

Punjab & Sind Bank (A Govt. of India Undertaking), H.O. HRD Department, NBCC Building Block 3, Kidwai Nagar, New Delhi – 110023 has officially released a notification dated 21/05/2026 for engagement of 635 Apprentices under the Apprentices Act, 1961 (as amended from time to time). The notification includes complete details on training seats, eligibility, selection process, State/UT-wise and district-wise vacancy breakup, PwBD reservation, application fee, and application instructions. Candidates are strongly advised to read the full notification before applying. The notification is available on the official Punjab & Sind Bank website.

  • Download Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Official Notification PDF

Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Overview

Punjab & Sind Bank is recruiting 635 Apprentice posts with Regular Graduation qualification, last date 06 June 2026.

Organization Name Punjab & Sind Bank (A Govt. of India Undertaking), H.O. HRD Department, New Delhi
Post Name Apprentice (under Apprentices Act, 1961)
Department Human Resource Development (HRD) Department, Punjab & Sind Bank
Total Training Seats 635 (across 29 States/UTs, including PwBD seats)
Stipend Rs. 12,300/- per month (no daily allowance / conveyance / travelling allowance)
Duration of Training 12 Months (On-the-Job Training; 10:00 AM to 05:00 PM)
Application Mode Online only (via NAPS portal + NATS portal + PSB website)
Selection Mode Merit-based on HSC / 10+2 marks (State-wise, District-wise, Category-wise) + Medical Examination
Mandatory Requirement Prior registration with 100% completed profile on NAPS portal (https://www.apprenticeshipindia.gov.in/) and NATS portal (https://nats.education.gov.in/)
Official Website https://punjabandsind.bank.in/

Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Important Dates

Event Date
