Opening of Online Registration of Application 21-05-2026
Last Date for Online Registration of Application (including Edit/Modification & Fee Payment) 06-06-2026
Age Calculated As On 01-05-2026
Educational Qualification Cut-off Date 06-06-2026
Document Verification / Joining Date To be notified after shortlisting

Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Vacancy Details

A total of 635 training seats have been announced for engagement as Apprentices under the Apprentices Act, 1961. These are distributed State/UT-wise across SC, ST, OBC, EWS, and UR categories with horizontal reservation for Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD). The number of training seats is provisional and may vary according to the actual requirement of the Bank. Candidates can apply for only one State and one District.

PwBD (Persons with Benchmark Disabilities) - Categories Eligible

Category Description

Hearing Impaired (HI) Deaf and Hard of Hearing
Orthopedically Challenged (OC) Locomotor Disability (OA, OL, BL, OAL), Cerebral Palsy, Leprosy Cured, Dwarfism, Acid Attack Victims, Muscular Dystrophy
Intellectual Disability (ID) Autism Spectrum Disorder, Specific Learning Disability, Mental Illness
Multiple Disabilities (MD) Combination of disabilities among VI, HI, OC, ID categories including Deaf-Blindness

Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the respective cut-off dates. Only candidates with 100% completed profile on both NAPS and NATS portals are eligible to apply through the Bank's website. Candidates can apply for only one State and one District and must not submit more than one application.

Criteria Details
Nationality / Citizenship Citizen of India / Subject of Nepal or Bhutan / Tibetan Refugee (came before 01-01-1962) / Person of Indian origin migrated from specified countries - with eligibility certificate from Govt. of India where applicable.
Portal Registration (Mandatory) 100% completed profile on NAPS portal (https://www.apprenticeshipindia.gov.in/) and NATS portal (https://nats.education.gov.in/) before applying on PSB website.
Educational Qualification (as on 06-06-2026) Regular Graduation in any discipline from a recognized University/Institute or equivalent qualification recognized by the Central Government.
Post Graduate Candidates Candidates who have completed Post Graduate qualification are NOT eligible.
Work Experience Candidates with work experience of 1 year or more after educational qualification are NOT eligible.
Years Since Qualifying Exam Candidates should not have completed 5 years after passing the qualifying examination.
Prior Apprenticeship Candidates who have already undergone or are currently undergoing Apprenticeship in any organisation under the Apprentices Act 1961 are NOT eligible.
Local Language Proficiency The candidate must be proficient in the local language (Reading, Writing, Speaking & Understanding) of the applied State/UT and must produce 8th/10th/12th or Graduate level certificate evidencing having studied the local language.
Aadhaar Requirement Every apprentice must have a valid Aadhaar number before engagement / contract signing.

Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit

Candidates must be a minimum of 20 years and maximum of 28 years as on 01-05-2026. Maximum age indicated is for Unreserved and EWS candidates. Relaxation in upper age limit is applicable as per Government of India guidelines for SC/ST/OBC/PwBD candidates.

Category Age Limit / Relaxation
General (UR) / EWS 20 to 28 years (as on 01-05-2026)
SC / ST 5 years relaxation
OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) 3 years relaxation
Persons With Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD) 10 years relaxation
Widows / Divorced Women / Women legally separated (not remarried) Up to 35 years (General/EWS), 38 years (OBC), 40 years (SC/ST)
Persons affected by 1984 riots 5 years relaxation

Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

Candidates are required to pay non-refundable Fees/Intimation Charges through online mode only. Bank transaction charges for online payment will have to be borne by the candidate. Fee once paid will NOT be refunded on any account nor can it be held in reserve for any other exam or selection.

Category Fees / Intimation Charges
SC / ST / PwBD Rs. 100/- + Applicable Taxes + Payment Gateway charges
General / EWS / OBC Rs. 200/- + Applicable Taxes + Payment Gateway charges

Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

Selection for engagement of apprentices will be merit-based on HSC/10+2 marks. No written examination is conducted. Final selection is subject to verification of eligibility, district-wise vacancy availability, and medical fitness. In case of a tie in marks, candidates will be ranked by age in descending order within their respective category, state, and district.

Stage 1 - Merit List (Based on HSC / 10+2 Marks)

Candidates who fulfill minimum eligibility criteria will be placed in a merit list in descending order of their 10+2 marks, drawn State-wise, District-wise, and Category-wise. No written examination is conducted for this recruitment. Candidates must select their preferred State and District in the application itself. In case of equal marks, tie-break is by age in descending order within the respective category, state, and district.

Stage 2 - Document Verification

Shortlisted candidates will be intimated through email only and must appear for document verification at their own expense. Original documents along with self-attested photocopies must be produced. Candidates failing to produce originals will not be allowed to proceed further.

Stage 3 - Medical Examination

Engagement of selected apprentices is subject to being declared medically fit as per the requirement of the Bank.

Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - District-wise Vacancy Details

Candidates must apply for one specific district within their chosen State. District-wise allotment will be subject to vacancy availability and administrative requirements of the Bank.

Uttarakhand 10 Dehradun, Haridwar 5, 5 Hindi
West Bengal 20 24 Paraganas North, 24 Paraganas South, Howrah, Kolkata 5, 5, 5, 5 Bengali / Nepali
Total   635  

Competition Level Analysis for Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026

The competition level for PSB Apprentice Recruitment 2026 is expected to be moderate to high. With only 635 seats spread across 29 States/UTs, the vacancy count is significantly lower than comparable bank apprenticeship drives such as SBI. Selection is purely merit-based on 10+2 marks without any written examination, which means candidates with high HSC scores - especially from states like Uttar Pradesh (100 seats), Punjab (90 seats), Bihar (40 seats), Maharashtra (30 seats), and Haryana (50 seats) - will face intense competition within their district and category.

Since there is no written exam, the effective competition depends entirely on the candidate's 10+2 percentage and age as a tie-breaker. Attrition at later stages will occur through local language proof verification, document verification, and medical fitness, which will reduce the final eligible pool. Candidates who do not have proof of studying the local language at 8th/10th/12th level are at a disadvantage as they must demonstrate local language proficiency separately.

Who should NOT apply for Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026?

  • Candidates looking for a permanent job; this is a 12-month apprenticeship only, with no guarantee of absorption or regular employment in the Bank.
  • Candidates who have already completed any Apprenticeship (in PSB or any other organisation) or are currently undergoing apprenticeship training under the Apprentices Act.
  • Candidates who have completed Post Graduate (PG) qualification - PG holders are explicitly not eligible.
  • Candidates with 1 year or more of work/job experience after completion of educational qualification.
  • Candidates who have completed more than 5 years after passing their qualifying examination.
  • Candidates outside the age band of 20–28 years as on 01-05-2026, except those eligible for notified relaxation under SC/ST/OBC/PwBD/widow/1984 riot victim categories.
  • Aspirants who do not hold a Regular Graduation degree from a recognized University/Institute by 06-06-2026.
  • Candidates not proficient in the local language of the State/UT they are applying for, and who cannot produce 8th/10th/12th or graduation-level certificate evidencing study of that language.
  • Candidates unwilling or unable to register with 100% completed profile on both NAPS (apprenticeshipindia.gov.in) and NATS (nats.education.gov.in) portals before applying - this is a mandatory prerequisite.
  • Candidates without a valid Aadhaar number, as it is mandatory before engagement/contract signing.
  • Candidates applying with a Ration Card or Learner's Driving License as ID proof - these are not accepted as valid identity documents for this process.

Common mistakes / What to check before applying Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026?

  • Not completing 100% profile on both NAPS and NATS portals before applying; incomplete portal profiles will render the candidate ineligible.
  • Applying for a wrong State or District where the candidate is not proficient in the local language and cannot produce language study certificates.
  • Entering the wrong category (SC/ST/OBC/EWS/UR); category cannot be changed after submission and any mismatch at document verification can lead to cancellation.
  • OBC candidates from the creamy layer applying under OBC reservation instead of UR; only Non-Creamy Layer OBC candidates with a valid, recent certificate are entitled to reservation.
  • Uploading blurred or incorrect photograph, signature, thumb impression or handwritten declaration (wrong size, format, or written by someone else) leading to rejection at scrutiny.
  • Using an invalid or inactive mobile number or email ID, resulting in failure to receive OTPs, shortlisting intimations, or further communication from the Bank.
  • Submitting multiple applications; only the last valid completed application will be retained and fees paid for other registrations will be forfeited.
  • Signing the application in CAPITAL LETTERS; signatures in capital letters are not acceptable and must match across all documents and correspondence.
  • Forgetting to note down the Enrollment ID generated on the NATS portal, which is mandatory to be mentioned while applying for training seats.
  • Not reading the clause that this is not regular employment, and later disputing about permanent absorption after the 12-month apprenticeship ends.

How to Apply for Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026

Candidates shall apply online through the Bank's website (https://punjabandsind.bank.in/) only. No other mode of application will be accepted. Before applying, candidates must mandatorily complete 100% registration on both the NAPS portal (https://www.apprenticeshipindia.gov.in/) and the NATS portal (https://nats.education.gov.in/). Candidates must also note down their Enrollment ID generated on the NATS portal as it is required while applying.

Documents Required to be Scanned and Uploaded:

  • Recent passport-size colour photograph (to be signed across by the candidate on printing)
  • Signature with black ink on white paper (Signatures in CAPITAL LETTERS will not be accepted)
  • Left Thumb Impression on white paper with black or blue ink
  • Handwritten Declaration in English on white paper with black ink - text: "I, _______ (Name of the candidate), hereby declare that all the information submitted by me in the application form is correct, true and valid. I will present the supporting documents as and when required."
  • Live photograph captured via webcam or mobile phone (to be uploaded at time of online application)
  • Image files must be in .jpg / .jpeg format; scanned documents must be in PDF format only

Documents to be Produced at the time of Document Verification / Joining:

  • Xth / Date of Birth certificate in support of Date of Birth
  • Printout of application form
  • All certificates and mark sheets of educational qualification from Standard Xth, XII, and Graduation onwards
  • Caste Certificate in prescribed Government of India format for SC/ST candidates
  • OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) Certificate in prescribed format, valid as on date of joining (issued within one year prior to joining); OBC Caste Name must tally with Central Government list
  • Income and Asset Certificate in prescribed format issued by competent authority, valid EWS certificate for the financial year, for EWS category candidates
  • Disability Certificate in prescribed format issued by District Medical Board for PwBD candidates
  • Age relaxation proof with valid documents (e.g., District Magistrate certificate for 1984 riot-affected persons)
  • Photo Identity Proof (PAN Card / Passport / Permanent Driving License / Voter’s Card / Bank Passbook with photograph / Aadhaar / E-Aadhaar / Identity Card issued by recognised College/University / Gazetted Officer certificate) - bearing the same name as on the application / call letter. Ration Card and Learner’s Driving License are NOT valid.
  • Two passport-size photographs similar to the one used during portal registration
  • Any other relevant documents in support of eligibility

NO DOCUMENT SHOULD BE DIRECTLY SENT TO THE BANK BEFORE OR AFTER THE PROCESS. Printout of Application Form is NOT to be sent to the Bank. Candidates must appear for document verification at their own expense.

Apply Online at: https://punjabandsind.bank.in/ → Home Page → Recruitment → Relevant Notification

For queries: Email [email protected] | All revisions/corrigendum (if any) will be hosted on the Bank's website only.

Important Instructions for Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • THIS IS NOT AN EMPLOYMENT IN THE BANK. Candidates engaged as Apprentice will NOT be treated as "employees" of the Bank and will not be entitled to receive any benefits available for Bank employees.
  • The Bank shall have no obligation to offer regular employment to any Apprentice who has completed the period of training, nor shall the Apprentice be obligated to accept employment. The Apprentice shall have no right to claim employment in the Bank after completion of training.
  • Candidates can apply for only one State and one District, and not more than one application should be submitted. In case of multiple applications, only the last valid (completed) application will be retained and fees for other registrations will stand forfeited.
  • Mandatory registration with 100% completed profile on both NAPS and NATS portals before applying on the Bank's website is required.
  • Candidates who had training or job experience of one or more years after completion of educational qualifications shall not be eligible for engagement as apprentice.
  • OBC candidates belonging to the "Creamy Layer" are not entitled to reservation/relaxation; they should apply as General/UR.
  • Change of address / email ID / phone number / caste / category will not be entertained after submission of the application.
  • Signatures in CAPITAL LETTERS will not be acceptable. The signature must be consistent across all documents and correspondence.
  • No daily allowance, conveyance allowance or travelling allowance will be paid to Apprentices. No overtime is permitted. Apprentices are entitled to only 1 casual leave per month (maximum 4 at a time).
  • The Contract of Apprenticeship shall terminate automatically on expiry of the 12-month training period. A non-performing apprentice will be given 7 days' notice. An apprentice wishing to terminate early must give 15 days' prior notice to the Bank.
  • Apprentices must devote their entire time exclusively to the Bank and shall not engage in any other commercial business, service, or advisory activity during the engagement period.
  • Apprentices must not disclose any financial information, data, concepts or internal communications relating to the Bank's business to any unauthorized person.
  • Canvassing in any form will be a disqualification.
  • Any resultant dispute arising out of this advertisement shall be subject to the sole jurisdiction of the Courts situated at Delhi.
  • Bank reserves the right to change/add/cancel the selection process or cancel the entire process at any stage without notice or refund of application fees.
  • All revisions/corrigendum (if any) will be hosted on the Bank's website only - candidates should visit regularly.

Bank of Baroda Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Overview

Organization Name Bank of Baroda (BOB), Mumbai
Advertisement No. BOB/HRM/APPRENTICE/ADVT/2026/01 dated 19/05/2026
Post Name Apprentice (under Apprentices Act, 1961)
Department Human Resource Management (HRM), Bank of Baroda
Conducted By BFSI Sector Skill Council of India (BFSI SSC)
Total Training Seats 5000 (across 35 States/UTs, including PwBD seats)
Stipend Rs. 15,000/- per month (no other allowances/benefits)
Duration of Training 12 months of On-the-Job Training
Application Mode Online only (via NATS / NAPS portals + BFSI SSC payment link)
Selection Mode Online Examination + Document Verification + Local Language Test
Mandatory Requirement Prior registration on NATS (https://nats.education.gov.in) and/or NAPS (https://www.apprenticeshipindia.gov.in)
Official Website https://bankofbaroda.bank.in/ and https://bfsissc.com

BOB Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Important Dates

Event Date
Date of Advertisement 19/05/2026
Opening of Online Application Portal 19-05-2026
Last Date for Online Application Submission 08-06-2026
Cut-Off Date for Eligibility (Age / Qualification) 01-05-2026
Online Examination Date To be notified on Bank's website and https://bfsissc.com
Document Verification / Language Proficiency Test Date To be notified after Online Examination result

BOB Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Vacancy Details

A total of 5000 training seats have been announced for engagement as Apprentices under the Apprentices Act, 1961. These are distributed State/UT-wise across SC, ST, OBC, EWS, and UR categories with horizontal reservation for Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD). The number of training seats is provisional and may vary according to the actual requirement of the Bank.

Sr. No. State/UT Total
1 Andaman & Nicobar Islands 1
2 Andhra Pradesh 188
3 Arunachal Pradesh 4
4 Assam 29
5 Bihar 179
6 Chandigarh 4
7 Chhattisgarh 122

9 Delhi 91

PwBD (Persons with Benchmark Disabilities) Reservation – Categories Eligible

PwBD Category Description
Visually Impaired (VI) Blindness and Low Vision
Hearing Impaired (HI) Deaf and Hard of Hearing
Orthopedically Challenged (OC) Locomotor Disability (OA, OL, BL, OAL), Cerebral Palsy, Leprosy Cured, Dwarfism, Acid Attack Victims and Muscular Dystrophy
Intellectual Disability (ID) Autism Spectrum Disorder, Specific Learning Disability and Mental Illness; & Multiple Disability
Multiple Disabilities Disability amongst clauses above including deaf-blindness in the posts identified for each disability

BOB Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the cut-off date (01-05-2026). Candidates must be registered on Government Apprenticeship Portals (NATS and/or NAPS) before applying. Only Indian citizens are eligible to apply.

Criteria Details
Registration Requirement Candidates must be registered on NAPS portal (https://www.apprenticeshipindia.gov.in) and/or NATS portal (https://nats.education.gov.in) before applying.
Educational Qualification Graduation Degree in any Discipline from a recognized University or any equivalent qualifications recognized by the Central Government. For NATS candidates: date of passing Graduation should not be more than 4 years preceding 01-05-2026.
Prior Apprenticeship Candidate should NOT have undergone Apprenticeship with Bank of Baroda or any other Establishment in the past, and should not currently be pursuing Apprenticeship Training with any Establishment.
Training / Job Experience Candidates who had training or job experience for a period of 1 year or more after completing educational qualification shall NOT be eligible.
State/UT Application Candidates can apply for engagement as apprentice in one State/UT only and must select up to 3 preferred training locations/centres from those notified within that State/UT.
Nationality Indian Citizens only.

BOB Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit

Candidates must be a minimum of 20 years and maximum of 28 years of age as on the cut-off date (01-05-2026). Age relaxation is applicable as per Government of India guidelines for reserved categories, subject to the condition that NAPS-registered candidates should be below 34 years as on 01-05-2026.

Category Age Relaxation (in years)
Scheduled Caste / Scheduled Tribe 5
Other Backward Classes (Non-Creamy Layer) 3
PwBD – General / EWS 10
PwBD – OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) 13
PwBD – SC / ST 15

BOB Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

Candidates are required to pay non-refundable Application/Intimation/Examination Charges/Fees to BFSI SSC through the secure payment link provided in the application form. The fee must be paid through ONLINE mode only. No other mode of payment will be accepted. Fee once paid will NOT be refunded on any account.

Category Total Fee / Charges
Scheduled Caste (SC) / Scheduled Tribe (ST) Candidates Rs. 200/- plus GST
Person with Benchmark Disability (PwBD) Candidates Rs. 200/- plus GST
General / EWS / OBC – Male Candidates Rs. 800/- plus GST
General / EWS / OBC – Female / Transgender Candidates Rs. 600/- plus GST

BOB Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

Selection for engagement of apprentices will be based on three stages - Online Examination, Document Verification, and Test of local language of the State. Candidates must qualify all three stages. Final selection is subject to medical fitness as per the Bank's requirements. A State-wise and Category-wise Wait List will be published for vacancies arising from non-acceptance or non-reporting by selected candidates; this wait list remains valid for 1 year from result declaration or until the next engagement exercise, whichever is earlier.

Stage 1 - Online Examination

S No Name of Test No. of Questions Maximum Marks Duration Medium of Test
1 General / Financial Awareness 25 25 60 Minutes Hindi / English
2 Quantitative & Reasoning Aptitude 25 25 Hindi / English
3 Computer Knowledge 25 25 Hindi / English
4 General English 25 25 English
Total 100 100  

There will be NO negative marks for wrong answers. The online examination will be remotely proctored - candidates can appear from any location of their choice using a smart mobile phone / laptop / desktop / tablet with an operational front camera, microphone, and stable Wi-Fi internet connection. SC/ST/OBC/PwBD candidates get 5% relaxation in minimum qualifying marks on aggregate. Merit list will be drawn State-wise and Category-wise in descending order of aggregate marks. Tie-break: higher age is preferred.

Stage 2 - Document Verification

Candidates qualifying the online examination will be called for Document Verification at a Bank-designated centre of the State/UT applied for, at their own expense. Bank of Baroda will not reimburse travelling expenses for Document Verification.

Stage 3 - Test of Local Language (State-wise)

Candidates who produce 10th or 12th standard mark sheet having studied the specified local language of the State/UT will not be required to undergo the language test. Candidates who fail to qualify the local language test will not be eligible for engagement as apprentice.

How to Apply for Bank of Baroda Apprentice Recruitment 2026

Applications must be submitted online only. No other mode of application will be accepted. Candidates must first mandatorily register themselves on the Government Apprenticeship portals - NATS (https://nats.education.gov.in) and/or NAPS (https://www.apprenticeshipindia.gov.in).

After completing registration on NAPS/NATS, eligible candidates will receive an email from [email protected] inviting them to fill the final "Application cum Examination Form" and make payment of the applicable fee/charges.

The application registration is considered complete only when fee/charges are deposited with BFSI SSC. Candidates must note down their Enrolment Id (NATS portal) and Apprentice Registration Code (NAPS portal) for all future correspondence.

Documents Required for Registration on NATS/NAPS Portals:

  • Colour scan copy of Aadhaar Card – Front & Back Page (JPEG, <1 MB)
  • Colour scan copy of PAN Card (JPEG, <1 MB)
  • Valid Personal Email ID and Mobile Number
  • Colour scan copy of Passport-size Photograph (JPEG, <1 MB)
  • Colour scan copy of Xth Marksheet (PDF, <1 MB)
  • Colour scan copy of XIIth Marksheet (PDF, <1 MB)
  • Colour scan copy of Degree Marksheet / Provisional Certificate (PDF, <1 MB)
  • Colour scan copy of Saving Bank Passbook / Cheque Leaf (PDF, <1 MB)
  • Colour scan copy of Signature (JPEG, <1 MB)

Documents to be Produced at the time of Document Verification / Language Proficiency Test:

  • Printout of valid Language Proficiency Test Intimation Letter/mail from Bank
  • Valid system generated printout of the online application / Registration / Enrollment form
  • Proof of Date of Birth (Birth Certificate from Competent Municipal Authority or SSLC / Std. X Certificate with DOB)
  • Photo Identity Proof (Government-issued ID)
  • Individual Semester/Year-wise Marksheets and Certificates for educational qualifications including final degree/diploma certificate
  • CGPA to Percentage conversion certificate (if grades awarded), issued by the appropriate authority
  • Caste Certificate in prescribed Government of India format (for SC/ST/OBC/EWS candidates) - OBC-NCL certificate must be issued within one year as on date of advertisement
  • Disability Certificate in prescribed format issued by District Medical Board (for PwBD candidates)
  • Any other relevant documents in support of eligibility

Documents to be produced at the venue of Document Verification / Language Test. Non-submission of requisite documents will debar candidature from further participation in the selection process. Candidates must appear at their own expense.

Apply Online at: https://bankofbaroda.bank.in/ and https://bfsissc.com

Important Instructions for Bank of Baroda Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • This is NOT regular employment or contract employment in the Bank. Apprentices will be TRAINEES only and not employees of Bank of Baroda.
  • The apprentice shall have no right to claim employment in the Bank after completion of training. Bank of Baroda has no obligation to offer regular employment to apprentices during or after the apprenticeship period.
  • Candidates can apply for engagement as apprentice in one State/UT only. Allotment to a different State/UT will not be considered under any circumstances.
  • Candidates must first register on NATS and/or NAPS portals before applying; application without portal registration is not valid.
  • The process of registration is complete only when applicable fee/charges are deposited with BFSI SSC.
  • No Ex-servicemen / Disabled Ex-Servicemen shall be engaged as an apprentice; there is no reservation for Ex-servicemen in this engagement.
  • OBC candidates belonging to the 'Creamy Layer' are not entitled to OBC reservation and age relaxation; they should apply as General / General (OC/HI/VI/ID) as applicable.
  • Change of category once submitted in the online application will not be permitted at any stage.
  • Candidates should submit only one application; in case of multiple submissions, only the last valid completed application will be considered and fee paid for earlier registrations will be forfeited.
  • The Apprentices are prohibited from joining / forming any Union Body.
  • All correspondence will be made only on the registered email ID; candidates should keep it active throughout the recruitment process. No hard copy intimation will be sent by post for Online Examination.
  • Candidates must appear for the selection process (Document Verification, Language Test) at their own cost; Bank will not reimburse travelling expenses.
  • Apprentices will not be provided with Transport / Hostel facility or any other residential accommodation during training.
  • Stipend of Rs. 15,000/- per month will be paid on completion of each month after adjusting Loss of Pay, if any. No other allowances/benefits are payable.
  • All amendments/corrigendum (if any) will be published only on the Bank's website (https://bankofbaroda.bank.in/) and https://bfsissc.com - candidates should visit regularly.
  • Any canvassing directly or indirectly shall disqualify the candidature. Use of unfair means, impersonation, or misbehavior will lead to disqualification and/or debarment.
  • Legal jurisdiction for any dispute arising out of this recruitment shall be Mumbai only.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

The State Bank of India (SBI) has officially announced the Trade Finance Officer (MMGS-II) - Specialist Cadre Officer Recruitment 2026 with 100 openings. The detailed notification was published on 13 May 2026 at sbi.co.in. Candidates who meet the age criteria of 23 to 32 years (relaxations applicable as per category) and hold a Graduate degree in any discipline from a recognized University along with Certification in Forex Operations from IIBF can now submit their online applications from 13 May 2026 to 02 June 2026. Direct links to download the notification and apply online are shared below.

FOLLOW US

SBI Trade Finance Officer Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

State Bank of India, Central Recruitment & Promotion Department, Corporate Centre, Mumbai has officially released Advertisement No. CRPD/SCO/2026-27/05 dated 13.05.2026 for recruitment to 100 posts of Trade Finance Officer (MMGS-II) on Regular Basis under Specialist Cadre Officers. The notification includes complete details on vacancies, eligibility, age limit, pay scale, job profile, selection process, application fee, and instructions to apply. The position shall remain in Specialist cadre and will have no provision for conversion into General cadre. Candidates are strongly advised to carefully read the full notification before applying online. The notification PDF is available on the Bank’s official career website.

  • Download SBI Trade Finance Officer SCO Recruitment 2026 Official Notification PDF

SBI Trade Finance Officer SCO Recruitment 2026 Overview

Organization Name State Bank of India (SBI), Central Recruitment & Promotion Department, Corporate Centre, Mumbai
Advertisement No. CRPD/SCO/2026-27/05 dated 13.05.2026
Post Name Trade Finance Officer (MMGS-II) – Specialist Cadre Officer, Regular Basis
Grade / Scale Middle Management Grade Scale – II (MMGS-II)
Total Vacancies 100 (Regular: 88 + Backlog: 12)
Pay Scale Rs. 64820-2340/1-67160-2680/10-93960
Suggested Place of Posting Hyderabad & Kolkata (indicative only; selected candidates may be posted anywhere in India)
Application Mode Online only (via https://sbi.co.in/web/careers/current-openings)
Selection Mode Shortlisting and Interview
Cut-off Date for Eligibility 30.04.2026
Official Website https://sbi.co.in

SBI Trade Finance Officer Recruitment 2026 Important Dates

Event Date
Date of Advertisement 13.05.2026
Opening of Online Registration & Payment of Fees 13-05-2026
Last Date for Online Registration & Payment of Fees 02-06-2026
Eligibility Cut-off Date (Age, Qualification, Experience) 30-04-2026
Interview Date To be notified on SBI website
Call Letter for Interview Sent by email / uploaded on Bank’s website only (No hard copy)

SBI Trade Finance Officer Recruitment 2026 - Vacancy Details

A total of 100 vacancies have been announced for the post of Trade Finance Officer (MMGS-II) on regular basis, comprising 88 regular vacancies and 12 backlog vacancies. The vacancies include horizontal reservations for PwBD candidates. EWS vacancies are tentative and subject to further directives of Government of India and outcome of any litigation. The position shall remain in Specialist cadre with no provision for conversion into General cadre.

Post Type Total
Trade Finance Officer (MMGS-II) Regular 88
Backlog 12
Total 100

SC: Scheduled Caste | ST: Scheduled Tribe | OBC: Other Backward Class | EWS: Economically Weaker Section | UR: Unreserved | VI: Visually Impaired | HI: Hearing Impaired | LD: Locomotive Disability | d&e: Specific Learning Disability, Mental Illness, Autism spectrum disorder, Multiple disabilities. PwBD reservation is horizontal and included in overall vacancy of respective parent category.

SBI Trade Finance Officer Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the cut-off date 30.04.2026. Teaching and training experience will not be counted for eligibility. The relevant experience certificate from the employer must specifically confirm that the candidate had experience in the related field as required. In cases where the degree/diploma certificate does not specify the field of specialization, a certificate from the concerned university/college specifically mentioning the specialization must be produced.

Educational Qualification and Experience

Qualification / Experience Details
Mandatory Educational Qualification Graduate (any discipline) from Government recognized University or Institution AND Certification in Forex Operations from IIBF (Certificate should be dated on or before 30.04.2026)
Preferred Qualification Certificate for Documentary Credit Specialists (CDCS) / Certificate in Trade Finance / Certificate in International Banking (Certificate should be dated on or before 30.04.2026)
Mandatory Experience Minimum 2 years’ post-essential academic qualification experience in Trade Finance processing in Supervisory/Official role in any Scheduled Commercial Bank
Specific Skills Required Outstanding communication, presentation and processing skills

SBI Trade Finance Officer Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit

Age is calculated as on 30.04.2026. Cumulative age relaxation will not be available. Candidates seeking age relaxation are required to submit copies of necessary certificate(s) at the time of interview, if shortlisted. No change in category of any candidate is permitted after online registration; no correspondence/email/phone will be entertained in this regard.

Category Age Limit / Relaxation
Unreserved (UR) 23 to 32 years (as on 30.04.2026)
OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) 3 years relaxation
SC / ST 5 years relaxation
PwBD (UR / EWS) 10 years relaxation
PwBD (OBC) 13 years relaxation
PwBD (SC / ST) 15 years relaxation

SBI Trade Finance Officer Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

Application fee and Intimation Charges are non-refundable and must be paid online through the payment gateway integrated with the application (Debit Card/Credit Card/Internet Banking). Transaction charges for online payment, if any, will be borne by the candidate. Application fee once paid will NOT be refunded on any account nor can it be adjusted for any other examination or selection in future. In case of multiple applications, fee paid for other registrations will stand forfeited.

Category Application Fee
General / EWS / OBC Rs. 750/- (Rupees Seven Hundred Fifty only)
SC / ST / PwBD Nil (No fee / intimation charges)

SBI Trade Finance Officer Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

Selection will be on the basis of Shortlisting and Interview. Merely fulfilling the minimum qualification and experience will not vest any right to candidate for being called for interview. The shortlisting committee constituted by the Bank will decide the shortlisting parameters. The decision of the Bank to call the candidates for interview shall be final. Interview will carry 100 marks; qualifying marks will be decided by the Bank. Merit list for selection will be prepared in descending order on the basis of scores obtained in interview only. In case of tie, candidates will be ranked according to their age in descending order. Intimation/Call Letter for Interview will be sent by email or uploaded on Bank’s website only - no hard copy will be sent.

Stage Details
Stage 1 Shortlisting (based on qualification, suitability, and experience as decided by the Bank’s shortlisting committee)
Stage 2 Interview (100 marks; qualifying marks decided by the Bank)
Merit List Prepared in descending order of interview scores; tie-breaking by age in descending order
Probation Period 6 months (as per Bank’s extant recruitment policy)

Grade, Pay Scale & Perquisites

Grade Probation Period Pay Scale Perquisites
Middle Management Grade Scale – II (MMGS-II) 6 Months Rs. 64820-2340/1-67160-2680/10-93960 DA, HRA, CCA, Provident Fund, NPS (Contributory Pension Fund), Leave Fare Concession (LFC), Medical Facility, and other perquisites as per rules in force from time to time

SBI Trade Finance Officer Recruitment 2026 - Job Profile & Key Responsibility Areas

The Trade Finance Officer (MMGS-II) position is in the Specialist cadre with no provision for conversion into General cadre. Suggested place of posting is Hyderabad and Kolkata; however, selected candidates may be posted anywhere in India at the sole discretion of the Bank. Requests for transfer/allotment to any centre other than Kolkata/Hyderabad shall not be entertained.

Area Details
Job Profile Trade Finance Document Scrutiny (LC/Collections/Bank Guarantees) for compliance with Bank/RBI/ICC/FEDAI/regulatory guidelines; Processing of Trade Finance and Forex transactions at a centralized location; Reconciliation of Trade Finance and Forex related entries in Bank’s Trade Finance, Forex and CBS systems and IDPMS/EDPMS; Any other tasks related to Bank’s Trade Finance and International Business back-office operations.
Key Responsibility Areas Accurate and efficient processing of all allotted Trade Finance and/or Forex transactions within benchmarked TAT; Tracking and ensuring periodic activities including maturities, payments, prompt follow-up, customer reconciliation; Timely reconciliation of related accounts/systems; Work closely with all stakeholders to maintain agreed customer TAT; Ensure adherence to all regulatory/bank/ICC/FEDAI guidelines; Participation in RCSA, BCOR, process review and training activities; Timely submission/compliance of all Audit Reports; Any other activity as directed by Team Leaders and Department/Branch Heads.

How to Apply for SBI Trade Finance Officer Recruitment 2026

Applications must be submitted online only through the link available on SBI’s official website https://sbi.co.in/web/careers/current-openings. No other mode of application will be entertained. Hard copy of application and other documents need not be sent to this office. The process of registration is completed only when fee is deposited with the Bank through online mode on or before the last date. Candidates are advised to apply online well before the closing date and not to wait till the last date to avoid any technical issues.

Candidates must upload all required documents at the time of online application; failure to upload documents will result in candidature not being considered for shortlisting/interview. After expiry of the window for online application, no change/correction/modification will be allowed under any circumstances.

Documents to be Uploaded at the Time of Online Application:

  • Recent Photograph & Signature (JPEG)
  • Detailed Resume (PDF)
  • ID Proof (PDF)
  • Proof of Date of Birth (PDF)
  • Caste Certificate & PwBD Certificate (if applicable) (PDF)
  • Mandatory Educational Certificates: Degree Certificate (PDF)
  • Mandatory Certification in Forex Operations from IIBF (PDF)
  • Experience Certificates (mandatory) (PDF)
  • Preferred Certificate, if any (PDF)
  • Biodata Form (duly filled, signed & scanned - available on Bank’s career website under the advertisement) (PDF)
  • Form-16 / Offer Letter / Latest Salary Slip from current employer (PDF)
  • No Objection Certificate (NOC) from present employer (mandatory if working in Government Institute/Public Sector Unit/Public Sector Bank) (PDF)
  • Mandatory Undertaking (format available on Bank’s career page against Adv. No. CRPD/SCO/2026-27/05) (PDF)

Apply Online at: https://sbi.co.in/web/careers/current-openings

Important Instructions for SBI Trade Finance Officer Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • Candidates must apply online only; hard copy of application and other documents need not be sent to SBI’s office.
  • The position of Trade Finance Officer (MMGS-II) shall remain in Specialist cadre and will have no provision for conversion into General cadre.
  • Teaching and training experience will not be counted for eligibility.
  • Candidates with any disciplinary proceedings pending or contemplated against them as on date will not be eligible to apply; an undertaking must be uploaded at the time of online application.
  • CIBIL: Candidates who have defaulted in repayment under any lending arrangement with Banks/NBFCs/Financial Institutions including credit card dues and have not regularized/repaid their outstanding till the date of issuance of offer of recruitment by the Bank shall not be eligible for appointment.
  • Candidates serving in Govt./Quasi Govt. offices/Public Sector undertakings/Nationalized Banks must submit NOC from their employer at the time of interview, failing which their candidature will not be considered.
  • In case of multiple applications for the same post, only the last valid (completed) application will be retained; fee paid for other registrations will stand forfeited.
  • OBC certificate containing the ‘non-creamy layer’ clause, issued during the period 01.04.2026 to the date of interview, must be submitted by OBC candidates if called for interview.
  • EWS candidates not in possession of “Income & Asset Certificate” for FY2025-26 on or before closure of online application date should apply under “UR Category” only.
  • Outstation candidates called for interview will be reimbursed airfare (economy class, shortest route, to-and-fro) up to a maximum of Rs. 10,000/- on submission of original flight tickets/invoice and boarding pass.
  • All changes/updates/revisions/corrigendum/results will be hosted on the Bank’s website only; no separate intimation will be issued.
  • Any legal proceedings arising out of this advertisement can be instituted only in Mumbai; Courts/Tribunals/Forums at Mumbai only shall have sole and exclusive jurisdiction.
  • Candidates are advised to keep their email ID/mobile number active till the declaration of result and issuance of call letters on final selection.
  • SBI does not endorse, authorize or associate with any external coaching platform, consultancy, individual or digital channel claiming to provide guaranteed selection or insider guidance. Candidates must rely solely on information available on SBI’s official career portal.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

The Himachal Pradesh State Co-operative Bank Ltd. (HPSCB) has officially announced the Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026 with 91 openings. The detailed notification was published on 09 May 2026 at www.hpscb.bank.in. Candidates who meet the age criteria of 18–45 years (with relaxations for reserved categories) and hold a Graduate degree in any discipline along with the required cooperative society experience can now submit their online applications from 09 May 2026 to 29 May 2026. Direct links to download the notification and apply online are shared below

FOLLOW US

HPSCB Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

The Himachal Pradesh State Co-operative Bank Ltd. (HPSCB), Head Office - The Mall, Shimla (H.P.) - 171001 has officially released the detailed notification for recruitment to 91 posts of Junior Clerks (quota for PACS/OCS of HP) on Regular Basis through IBPS, Mumbai. The notification includes complete details on vacancies, eligibility, selection process, examination pattern, syllabus, PwBD guidelines, fee, and application instructions. Candidates are strongly advised to download and read the full notification before applying online. The notification PDF is available on the official HPSCB website.

  • Download HPSCB Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026 Official Notification PDF

HPSCB Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026 Overview

Organisation Name The Himachal Pradesh State Co-operative Bank Ltd. (HPSCB), Shimla - 171001
Post Name Junior Clerk (quota for PACS/OCS of HP)
Conducting Body IBPS, Mumbai (Online Examination conducted through IBPS)
Quota Type PACS Quota (Trained Secretaries of Primary Agricultural Cooperative Societies of HP) and OCS Quota (Employees of Other Cooperative Societies and PACS employees not covered as Trained Secretaries)
Total Vacancies 91 (PACS Quota: 74 + OCS Quota: 17)
Pay Scale Minimum Pay of Rs. 19,900/- (Level-2) + admissible allowances per month (Regular Basis)
Application Mode Online only (via HPSCB website - www.hpscb.bank.in)
Selection Mode Online Examination (150 marks, 150 minutes) + Document Verification/Scrutiny (No Personal Interview)
Mandatory Requirement Bonafide Himachali for all reserved categories; valid experience certificate from cooperative society (PACS: min. 3 years; OCS: min. 5 years) countersigned by Assistant Registrar, Cooperative Societies of H.P.
Official Website https://www.hpscb.bank.in

Event Date

Date of Declaration of Result for Online Examination Around 15 days after conduct of examination
Age Calculated As On 01-01-2026

HPSCB Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026 - Vacancy Details

A total of 91 vacancies have been announced for the post of Junior Clerk under two quota categories - PACS Quota (74 posts) and OCS Quota (17 posts). These are distributed across General (UR), EWS, SC, ST, OBC, Ex-Servicemen, IRDP, WFF, and PwBD categories. All reserved category benefits apply only to Bonafide Himachali candidates. Number of posts may vary depending on actual requirement at the time of final selection.

HPSCB Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on 01-01-2026. All reserved category candidates must be Bonafide Himachalis. Reservation benefits are applicable only to Bonafide Himachali candidates. OBC candidates must submit a valid Non-Creamy Layer certificate from competent authority. Candidates belonging to SC/ST/OBC/EWS/PwBDs/Ex-SM and other reserved categories of HP may apply against unreserved/general category posts but will not be eligible for age relaxation or fee concession in such cases. Candidates must also ensure they are not directly or indirectly a defaulter of their society.

Experience certificates must be issued by the concerned Inspector of Society and countersigned by the Assistant Registrar, Cooperative Societies of H.P. with authentic certification. Such certificate must be supported by: (i) Copy of resolution of the managing committee for initial appointment, and (ii) Copy of relevant pages of statutory audit report/note for prescribed years showing payment of wages/salary from the society account.

Educational Qualification

Post / Quota Essential Qualifications
Junior Clerk (PACS Quota - Trained Secretaries of PACS of HP) (i) Graduate in any discipline from a recognized University (Candidates must have passed Matriculation and 10+2 from a School/Institution within Himachal Pradesh - not applicable for Bonafide Himachalis); (ii) Minimum 3 years continuous service as Secretary as defined in Rule 2(vii) of H.P. Cooperative Societies Rules, 1971 of Primary Agriculture Credit Society; (iii) All employees of PACS covered under definition of "Trained Secretaries" are eligible; (iv) Candidate should not be a defaulter of his/her society
Junior Clerk (OCS Quota - Employees of Other Cooperative Societies and PACS employees not covered as Trained Secretaries) (i) Graduate in any discipline from a recognized University (Matriculation and 10+2 from HP school - not applicable for Bonafide Himachalis); (ii) Minimum 5 years continuous service in a cooperative society; (iii) All employees of other cooperative societies and PACS employees not covered under definition of "Trained Secretaries" are eligible; (iv) Candidate should not be a defaulter of his/her society

Types of PACS and OCS covered under this recruitment:

Quota Type Societies Covered
PACS (Primary Agricultural Credit Societies) Cooperative Multipurpose Societies Ltd.; Gram Service Cooperative Societies Ltd.
OCS (Other Cooperative Societies) Fruit Growers and Marketing Society Ltd.; Marketing and Consumer Cooperative Societies Ltd.; Cooperative Consumer Store; Thrift & Credit Society Ltd.; All other Cooperative Societies registered under HP Cooperative Societies Act, 1968

HPSCB Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit

Candidates must be above 18 years and below 45 years of age as on 01-01-2026. Candidates born between 02-01-1981 and 01-01-2008 (both days inclusive) are eligible. Age relaxation is applicable only to Bonafide Himachali candidates of the respective reserved categories. Selected PwBD candidates will have to submit a Medical Certificate from a competent authority confirming physical fitness to perform duties.

Category Age Relaxation
General (UR) 18 to 45 years (as on 01-01-2026)
SC of HP 5 years relaxation (max 50 years)
ST of HP 5 years relaxation (max 50 years)
OBC of HP 5 years relaxation (max 50 years)
EWS of HP As per HP Govt. norms
Physically Handicapped (PwBD) 5 years relaxation (minimum 40% disability required)
Ward of Freedom Fighter (WFF) of HP 5 years relaxation
Ex-Servicemen (Reserved Vacancies) Age at time of joining military service should not exceed upper age limit; present age must be less than 58 years (Maximum relaxation: 12 years)
Ex-Servicemen (Unreserved Vacancies) Actual age minus approved military service must not exceed prescribed maximum age by more than 3 years (Maximum relaxation: 12 years)

HPSCB Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

Candidates applying for the post of Junior Clerk are required to pay a non-refundable application fee as specified below. The fee must be paid online through the HPSCB application portal. Fee once paid will not be refunded under any circumstances. Bank transaction charges for online payment will have to be borne by the candidate.

Category Application Fee
General (UR) and OBC of H.P. Rs. 1,000/- (Rupees One Thousand only) - Non-refundable
EWS of H.P. (Economically Weaker Sections) Rs. 800/- (Rupees Eight Hundred only) - Non-refundable
SC / ST / IRDP / BPL / ANTODAYA of HP Rs. 800/- (Rupees Eight Hundred only) - Non-refundable
Female Candidates of All Categories of HP Rs. 800/- (Rupees Eight Hundred only) - Non-refundable

HPSCB Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

The selection process for HPSCB Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026 consists of 2 stages - Online Examination (150 marks, 150 minutes, conducted through IBPS Mumbai) followed by Document Verification/Scrutiny. There is no personal interview. Candidates up to 1.75 times of vacant posts will be called for document verification. Final selection will be made strictly on the basis of merit in the online examination after document verification. Candidates must score minimum 40% marks in the online examination to be shortlisted for document verification. Candidates declared successful who do not personally attend document verification will have their candidature cancelled.

Stage 1 - Online Examination (150 Marks, 150 Minutes)

Sl. No. Subject No. of Questions Marks Duration
1 Reasoning 30 30 150 Minutes (2½ Hours) - No separate time limit per section
2 Numerical Ability 30 30
3 English 30 30
4 General Awareness (including HP General Knowledge) 30 30
5 H.P. Cooperative Societies Act and H.P. Cooperative Societies Rules 30 30
Total 150 150  

Stage 2 - Document Verification / Scrutiny

Shortlisting Ratio Candidates up to 1.75 times of vacant posts will be called for document verification
Minimum Qualifying Marks 40% of total marks (i.e., minimum 60 out of 150) in online examination
No Negative Marking There is NO negative marking in the online examination
Question Paper Type Objective type multiple choice (MCQ) - bilingual (English and Hindi)

Tie-Breaking Rules

1st Tie-Break Preference to candidates possessing higher academic qualifications
2nd Tie-Break Preference to candidates belonging to the State of H.P.

HPSCB Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026 - Exam Syllabus

The Online Examination consists of 5 sections - Reasoning, Numerical Ability, English, General Awareness, and H.P. Cooperative Societies Act and Rules - each carrying 30 marks (total 150 marks, 150 minutes). The level of examination and syllabus is in accordance with the minimum educational qualification criteria i.e. Graduation level. The question paper is bilingual (English and Hindi) and is of objective type MCQ. There is no negative marking. Preference will be given to candidates possessing knowledge of customs, traditions, languages, and culture of Himachal Pradesh and the test shall include questions on such subjects.

Section Topic Key Coverage Marks
Section 1 Reasoning Logical Reasoning, Analytical Reasoning, Verbal and Non-Verbal Reasoning 30
Section 2 Numerical Ability Quantitative Aptitude, Number Systems, Arithmetic, Data Interpretation 30
Section 3 English Reading Comprehension, Grammar, Vocabulary, Sentence Completion 30
Section 4 General Awareness (including HP GK) General Awareness, Current Affairs, HP General Knowledge, Customs, Traditions, Languages and Culture of Himachal Pradesh 30
Section 5 H.P. Cooperative Societies Act & H.P. Cooperative Societies Rules Provisions of HP Cooperative Societies Act and HP Cooperative Societies Rules 30

How to Apply for HPSCB Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026

Applications must be submitted online only through the Bank's website www.hpscb.bank.in by clicking the "APPLY ONLINE" option. Hard copy applications or applications by any other mode will not be accepted. The online application window is open from 09-05-2026 to 29-05-2026. Candidates must register on the portal, make fee payment online, and upload all required documents. No change will be possible after clicking the "COMPLETE REGISTRATION" button. Candidates are advised to keep their valid personal email ID and mobile number active till the completion of the entire recruitment process.

Before applying, candidates must have ready: scanned photograph (4.5 cm × 3.5 cm), signature (with black ink on white paper), left thumb impression (on white paper with black or blue ink), and a hand-written declaration in English on white paper with black ink. Signature in CAPITAL LETTERS will NOT be accepted. The hand-written declaration text is: "I, _______ (Name of the candidate), hereby declare that all the information submitted by me in the application form is correct, true and valid. I will present the supporting documents as and when required."

Documents Required to be Produced at Document Verification (when called):

  • Graduation degree certificate and mark sheets from recognized University
  • Matriculation and 10+2 certificates from HP school/institution (if applicable)
  • Experience Certificate countersigned by Assistant Registrar, Cooperative Societies of H.P. - with copy of managing committee resolution for initial appointment and statutory audit report pages (3 years for PACS quota; 5 years for OCS quota)
  • Age proof (10th/12th class mark sheet/certificate)
  • Bonafide Himachali Certificate from competent authority (for reserved category candidates)
  • SC/ST/OBC/EWS/PwBD/WFF/IRDP certificate from competent authority (if applicable)
  • Non-Creamy Layer (NCL) Certificate (for OBC candidates) - in latest prescribed format
  • EWS Income and Asset Certificate issued by Deputy Commissioner/SDO (Civil)/Tehsildar (for EWS candidates) - family income below Rs. 4 Lakhs per annum
  • PwBD Certificate (minimum 40% disability) + Medical Certificate from competent authority confirming fitness for duty (for PwBD candidates)
  • Discharge Certificate (for Ex-Servicemen candidates)
  • WFF Certificate (for Ward of Freedom Fighter candidates)
  • IRDP/BPL/Antodaya Certificate countersigned by Block Development Officer (if applicable)
  • NOC and Good Character Certificate from current employer (for candidates in regular/confirmed service)
  • e-Receipt and printout of online application form

Apply Online at: https://ibpsreg.ibps.in/hpscbmar26/

HPSCB Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026 - Application Link and Form Details

The online application for HPSCB Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026 is to be filled and submitted through the Bank's official website. The step-by-step process is as follows:

Step Action Details
Step 1 Visit Bank's Website Go to www.hpscb.bank.in → Click "APPLY ONLINE" → Choose "Click here for New Registration" → Enter Name, Contact Details, and Email ID → Note the Provisional Registration Number and Password generated
Step 2 Fill the Application Form Fill all personal details, category, qualification, and experience details. Use "SAVE AND NEXT" to save data if unable to complete in one go. Details must match exactly as in certificates/mark sheets.
Step 3 Upload Documents Upload: (1) Photograph (4.5×3.5 cm, 20-50 KB, JPG); (2) Signature (black ink, 10-20 KB, JPG); (3) Left Thumb Impression (black/blue ink on white paper, 20-50 KB, JPG); (4) Hand-written Declaration (black ink on white paper, English only, 50-100 KB, JPG). Capture live webcam photo as required.
Step 4 Preview and Complete Registration Click "Preview Tab" to verify all details → Modify if required → Click "COMPLETE REGISTRATION" only after verifying everything is correct. No changes allowed after this step.
Step 5 Pay Application Fee Click "Payment" Tab → Pay online via Debit Card (RuPay/Visa/MasterCard/Maestro), Credit Card, Internet Banking, IMPS, or Mobile Wallets → Wait for server intimation (do not press Back/Refresh) → Download e-Receipt
Step 6 Submit and Print Click "Submit" → Take printout of e-Receipt and online application form with fee details → Download Call Letter (around 7 days before exam) from www.hpscb.bank.in using Registration Number/Roll Number and Password/Date of Birth

For queries: [email protected]

Apply Online at: https://ibpsreg.ibps.in/hpscbmar26/

Important Instructions for HPSCB Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • All reserved category benefits including age relaxation and fee concession apply only to Bonafide Himachali candidates.
  • Candidates must possess valid experience certificate from their cooperative society - false experience certificates will result in summary cancellation of candidature irrespective of qualifying the online examination.
  • Experience certificates must be issued by the concerned Inspector of Society and countersigned by the Assistant Registrar, Cooperative Societies of H.P. supported by managing committee resolution and statutory audit report pages.
  • No hard copy applications will be accepted - online submission only via www.hpscb.bank.in.
  • Application fee is non-refundable; fee once paid will not be refunded under any circumstances.
  • Signature in CAPITAL LETTERS and hand-written declaration not in candidate's own handwriting or not in English will be treated as invalid.
  • Candidates already in regular (confirmed) service must submit NOC and good character certificate from current employer at document verification - absence of NOC will result in cancellation of candidature.
  • There is NO negative marking in the online examination; minimum 40% marks required to qualify.
  • Candidates must appear in person at document verification - non-appearance will lead to cancellation of candidature without notice.
  • OBC candidates must submit a valid Non-Creamy Layer certificate; EWS candidates must have family gross annual income below Rs. 4 Lakhs per annum.
  • PwBD candidates must have minimum 40% degree of disability and must submit Medical Certificate from competent authority confirming fitness for duty at selected post.
  • Wards of Ex-Servicemen are NOT eligible for posts advertised for Ex-Servicemen.
  • Selected candidates will be on probation for a period of two years (extendable up to maximum 3 years).
  • No TA/DA is admissible for appearing in the online examination.
  • Selected candidates will be posted anywhere in the area of operation of H.P. State Cooperative Bank in Himachal Pradesh.
  • For any dispute related to selection process, the decision of the Registrar, Co-operative Societies of Himachal Pradesh shall be binding and final; jurisdiction shall be Shimla (HP) only.
  • Candidates are advised to visit www.hpscb.bank.in regularly for all updates regarding the recruitment process.

How to Calculate Your Score Using SBI Clerk Mains Answer Key 2026 ?

Use this formula to estimate your score:

Total Score = (Correct Answers × Marks per Question) – (Wrong Answers × Negative Marking)

Marking Scheme for SBI Clerk Mains Exam:

  • Correct Answer: +1 mark (most sections), +2 marks for Computer Aptitude
  • Wrong Answer: -1/4 mark (negative marking)
  • Unattempted: 0 marks

Candidates can check their result and download scorecard using Registration Number/Roll Number & Password/DOB. Check qualifying status, provisional allotment, and next steps below.

FOLLOW US

Are you waiting for IBPS Clerk Mains Result 2025-26? Great news! The Institute of Banking Personnel Selection has officially published the result for Clerk (Customer Service Associate) posts today (2nd March 2026). Thousands of candidates who appeared in the Mains examination held on 29th November & 2nd December 2025 can now verify their qualifying status and provisional allotment online.

This article provides complete information about IBPS Clerk Mains Result 2025-26 including direct download links, provisional allotment, expected cutoff marks, scorecard details, and step-by-step instructions to check results.

How to Check IBPS Clerk Mains Result 2025-26?

Candidates can download their IBPS Clerk Mains Result 2025-26 scorecard by following this simple process:

  1. Visit the official website: www.ibps.in
  2. Click on the “CRP Clerical” or “Current Openings” section.
  3. Find and click on the link for “Result of Online Main Examination for CRP-CSA-XV (Provisional Allotment)”.
  4. Enter your Registration Number/Roll Number and Password/Date of Birth.
  5. Input the captcha code and submit.
  6. Your result, scorecard, and provisional allotment status will appear on the screen.
  7. Download and print the scorecard for future reference.

IBPS Clerk Merit List 2025-26 - What’s Inside?

The IBPS Clerk provisional allotment list 2025-26 is a comprehensive document containing details of all provisionally allotted candidates. IBPS prepares the list based on mains marks (prelims qualifying), category, merit-cum-preference, and vacancies.

Merit List Contains:

  • Registration/Roll Number of qualified candidates
  • Candidate Name
  • Category (Gen/OBC/SC/ST/EWS)
  • Total Marks Obtained (Mains)
  • Provisional Allotment Bank
  • Qualifying Status & Remarks

Types of Lists Released:

  1. Provisional Allotment List: Selected candidates allotted to banks
  2. Category-Wise Merit: Separate considerations for reservations
  3. Reserve List: May be released later if needed

IBPS Clerk Scorecard 2025-26 - Information Breakdown

Your IBPS Clerk scorecard 2025-26 will display the following information:

Candidate Name As mentioned in application form
Registration/Roll Number Unique identification number
Exam Date 29th November & 2nd December 2025
Section-Wise Marks Individual section scores (Reasoning, English, Quant, GA, Computer)
Total Marks Obtained Out of 200 (converted to 100 for allotment)
Qualifying Status Qualified for Provisional Allotment / Not Qualified
Provisional Allotment Allotted Bank (if qualified)
